I will not order again. Waste of money. Review 13: rating 2.0. Soppy cups Would not recommend they leak . I had to put a small hole in it myself because it didn’t have one and I ordered 2 so both had to be punched. My daughter got 2 also and hers had the same problem. These worked great for about a month... Not sure what happened they started leaking Alot... Pouring out. I threw them out as they would have messes on the boys shirts... Good while they lasted. Review 18: rating 2.0. Leaky mess Initially very happy with these cups. But after a few months they leak quite a bit even as my son is drinking. You have to push the spout into the screw top and its wedged in tight. Over time I realized the pushing with my finger stretched out a little vent hole so everything leaks from there. Still looking for a leak proof cup.

Initial review: These work pretty well but if left laying on its side, it sometimes does leak a little bit. These are 100% better tha... Review 19: rating 1.0. Hard to suck I cant suck threw so my 1 year old definitely cant . Very disappointed Review 20: rating 1.0. persona: This customer values practicality, reliability, and subtle quality improvements over competitors, prioritizing leak resistance in sippy cups, clean ingredients and manageable texture in snacks, and balanced moisture levels with a soft, cloth-like feel in wipes—seeking products that perform consistently well in real-life use without excessive mess or waste.
reference_output: rating 4.0. pretty good! But not great If you’re looking for a sippy cup of this spout style, this one is pretty good!! It does leak a little from the spout from time to time depending on how my 15 month old handles it (if he pushed the spout agains something ir aggressively beats it up obviously) but overall doesn’t leak if it’s just being held and walked around with or even if it tips over. We just decided he should be mainly using straw cups so we kind of phased these out. Initially it helped us to transition him off a bottle though. He refused it at first for a couple days with his milk in it but easily adapted after just a couple days when he realized that was his only option. It would be nice to have the handles on it for maybe younger babies but he held it just fine.

Also on a normal non gfi outlet, it has a flimsy attachment that allows for enoug leverage to be pulled upen by a baby. Don't buy this unless you want your child to be injured. Review 19: rating 1.0. The plastic is also so thin that if you have to use one middle screw to attach it, you could pull the top part out and stick your fingers in (it is not at all baby proof, and probably worse than not having anything at all). Review 20: rating 1.0. Cheap These are terrible, I'm my opinion. They were confusing to install and are so loose and cheap-feeling the babies can pull them off.
persona: This customer prioritizes safety, functionality, and reliability in baby products, seeking solutions that address specific developmental challenges like sleep, mobility, and environmental hazards, and values responsive customer service and long-term usability over price or convenience features.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Great baby proofing for a baby who can outsmart the parent ! I always go for the outlet covers that just plug in to block kids from getting their fingers in them. My 3rd child, which is 11 months, quickly realized he wasn’t able to mess with those outlets but instead went for the outlets that he can just pull the plugs out of the wall completely. Most of my outlets like that are blocked but due to Christmas we had to rearranged which left one outlet that is in use open and I had to pull my couch down the wall to block it which makes it hard to get to so I looked into this. It was easy to put on, unfortunately I wasn’t able to screw it in from the top and bottom with how my outlet is set up but the middle screw was perfect and holds it in place sturdy. The cover is a hassle to open without taking the whole thing off. But I guess that means if I have trouble my kids will more than likely have trouble so it’s better than having my couch where I don’t want it sitting and having a hard time swapping what needs to be used and also it separates the cords at the bottom so they don’t wind up being mixed up. And a plus is the price of this and getting 2 ! I’ll definitely be buying more.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Great Cups I bought these after I tried 2 other brands of training cups, which my twins really hated and refused to drink from.

The big plus about this cup is that you can drink from it without raising it, which is much easier for babies. It doesn't spill and the straw is very easy to use. My twins love it! great purchase. Review 2: rating 5.0. our baby loves them Our baby is a picky cup user & he loves these from the first time I introduced it. After buying so many cups, I decided to get these per a coworker's recommendation. Review 3: rating 1.0. Still Can't Figure These Out! If I can't figure these cups out, how can my little one figure them out? He would actually have to bite the straw in exactly the right way to get anything out of this sippy. The instructions were vague and I plan on either returning these or donating them, although I would hate to bring this frustration onto anyone else! Review 4: rating 2.0. They Leak All the Time Am I doing something wrong? Every time I put cold liquid (milk or juice) into these within ten minutes it is leaking. A vacuum effect pushes the liquid up through the straw and bubbles out the top. It is so annoying. I have juice all over the side of my diaper bag, all over the car seat cup holder and all over any surface my daughter sets her cup down. She loves drinking out of the cup. The straw is great for a teething toddler. She chews on it. But the leaking... Review 5: rating 2.0. Not what I expected I have been using the Nuby flexi-straw bottles for my son since he was 9 months old. I thought I was ordering the Nuby Flip Top bottle and was surprised when I got these (my bad). I was going to return them but shipping would have eaten up the cost of the refund so I kept them. If you get the tops on just right, they don't leak and work perfectly. But it's not so easy to get everything lined up exactly. I read other complaints about the straw. My son has no ... Review 6: rating 5.0. Best Sippy For Your Money I've been on the hunt for an honestly good sippy cup for a long time now, and this is EXACTLY what I've been looking for. My son just turned one and he LOVES them. I found 1 at Walmart on sale, and when I went to look for them I discovered they're not widely available, which is a bummer. This is a no-spill sippy cup that actually doesn't spill if you screw the cap on straight. If you put any liquids in it that will bubble, of course you'll get some leakage f... Review 7: rating 5.0. One of our favorite I LOVE these straw cups for our 1 yr old. Although I have a hard time drinking from it, our daughter has no problems using the straw LOL. It was the first straw cup she was willing to drink milk from. What I love most is the fact there are only 3-4 parts to clean and it's incredibly easy to reassemble. We also have the straw cups that flip up and down, but they're such a pain to take apart and put back together.

I don't understand why some parents gave ... Review 8: rating 3.0. colors may vary! I did see the disclaimer that 'colors may vary', but I chanced it. I received pink. I have a boy. I actually ordered 2 sets, so we kept the blue set. The straw is the kind that the child has to bite in order to open the valve at the tip. My son has only tried it a couple of times, but he doesn't get it yet (11 months old). He has previously used the Playtex straw cup where the valve is further down inside the lid and you just need to suck normally to get the liquid o... Review 9: rating 3.0. 18-month old loves them. Careful though, parts will break off from straw We have these for our daughter. She really likes them. They work pretty well. I only have two complaints about them:
1) My daughter will mash on the soft, dome top and watch the ensuing fountain of milk/water/juice. I've gotten to where I was going with a soaking wet child on more than one occasion.
2) We have noticed that after a while, the cups will leak from the straw if left on the side. Up... Review 10: rating 5.0. Great transitional sippy cup. Great for making the move to big kid cups. We chose these because the cup has to be upright vs. upside down like bottles/traditional sippy cups. This made it easier for our daughter to know the difference, as well as making life outside the home (restaurants with straws) easier. Now that she's off the bottle completely, she still,knows to keep her cups upright to avoid spills so that it makes big cup drinking a little easier.
The cups seem to hold ... Review 11: rating 5.0. Great for milk Easy to clean, not too many parts, NEVER leaks. We use these every day for milk with our toddlers. I was sick of the "jigsaw" puzzles of straws and other parts with sippy cups and also liked how you can see what is in these cups. We had character ones before, playtex, and I would find them picking up a sippy later in the day..... I would think it was water in the sippy cup then I would hear "yummy milk" and I would freak out because the milk must be spo... Review 12: rating 2.0. Yikes! Plastic taste Leeches off Cup Unfortunately, I had to throw these cups out. Very hard to sip out of the straw, which was perhaps intentional -- less choke risk for babies?? -- but kind of annoying for lots of kids, plus could not get rid of the chemical taste the cups gave off. Was a little alarming, frankly. Review 13: rating 5.0. Great for Kids! My baby was having trouble learning to use a sippy cup. She couldn't figure out how to tilt it up. I handed her this cup and she was instantly drinking all on her own! I LOVE THESE CUPS! Review 14: rating 5.0. Perfect for 1 year old! My son is almost 18 months and this is the sippy cup I used to get him off the bottle around13 months. These cups have taken a beating over the last 5-6 months!! And they've held up great. I had to get rid of 1 out of the 6 we started with because the seal at the top of the straw broke somehow so we had leaking issues, but other than that I couldn't be happier. They're very hard to find in stores and Nuby changed the style recently. The new style is not n... Review 15: rating 5.0. baby loves these easy for my baby to use. She loves that she is able to watch us now when drinking water. Other sippy cups that she has she has to bend her head back to get a drink. Has a nice soft drinking spout that she likes to sometimes munch on too. Review 16: rating 5.0. Had it for 4 months and still love it! I am a big review reader and was skeptical when buying this product and bought a couple other kinds as well. This is by far my favorite. My 18 month old didn't have a problem figuring out the straw and you just won't know that till they try it. It is cheap so no huge loss if it doesn't work for them. As far as biting off the straw and choking, she carries it around in her mouth by the straw sometimes and it has held together. To our dismay she t... Review 17: rating 5.0. Sippy cups As expected, they are Nuby sippy cups, which is what I wanted and why I ordered these. I have others, they work good and I needed a couple more. Review 18: rating 5.0. Perfect for kids of all ages Easily used by my youngest of 9 months and oldest of 3 years. Leak proof and have less inner workings to clean. Hold a good amount. Review 19: rating 1.0. Don't Like I bought these for my 1 1/2 year old. He loves strays so I was really excited. However with this product he has to bit the straw and suck at the same time to get water to come out. Seems a bit challenging for that young. I'll probably keep them on hand for when he get's older. Review 20: rating 2.0. :( Got the smaller version of these with the handles and flip tops and loved them so I got two of these and hate them!!! My girls won't even drink out of them because they are so hard to suck water out of and then when you do get a little water the soft top on them collapses, I was very disappointed!
persona: This customer values practical, multi-functional products that solve specific parenting challenges, especially around feeding and portability, and is drawn to items that offer creative solutions, compatibility with existing products, and engaging features for children, while being willing to overlook minor usability flaws if the overall utility and design are strong.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Great Sippy Cup My son loves these.  He had been struggling with regular sippy cups but has no problem adjusting to these.  The only struggle we have is when the level gets low, he still likes to lift it like drinking from a regular cup- our solution to this though is just to remove the straw and it still works great!  They truly are no-spill with the exception of a small drop that may get caught in the top of the straw and flies out as my son shakes the cup around- but a drop is much better than a leak!  The only negative is that by choosing the "Colors May Vary" option we got a pink cup and a purple cup- but it was a cheaper price and doesn't really bother us, but in light of an honest review, I thought I would mention it for people who may care.

Buy a regural pacifier clip more hygienic and cost effect... Review 19: rating 2.0. I have a lot about these that I don't like. I don't like that the pacifier cannot be removed to be cleaned. (GROSS)
When babies get teeth they start to chew on them, so they should be able to be replaced without ... Review 20: rating 5.0. It seemed an expensive luxury, but we use these for naps and nighttime only, and they have been a wonderful comfort. Babies can hold them, cuddle them and grab them much more easily when they lose them in the night.

I have washed them in the washing machine and dishwasher, and they hold up great.

Does the job until you go to store it then not so much Use this to stop our dogs from getting into the basement. It does the job well. Main complaint is it never retracts the full way on its own. You end up having to coach it back in by messing with the roll. Review 6: rating 1.0. Doesn't cover a 71" opening Our opening is 70" and this gate does not cover it, even though it's listed as covering 71". Review 7: rating 2.0. Decent at best but I wouldn’t use near steps or stairs. reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Awesome gate !! This gate is really awesome !! I have never imagined that a gate could bring so much beauty to the room. I got so many compliments from friends & family.

If you have a narrow door it will stick out very far. If your door is wide it will stick out slightly less. It took up way too much space- we shipped it back. Review 4: rating 4.0. reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Expensive but worth it Our daughter is allergic to cow's milk and soy and we needed to find a formula to help her gain some weight. She loves this formula and calls it her "special milk." She had also started growing again in height and weight. She tells us that this formula makes her tummy "feel good." Review 2: rating 5.0. My 2 year old loved the taste. My daughter loved this.
She is underweight and the doctor recommended.
I am so pleased. Review 3: rating 5.0. Great taste My 4 year old loves it Review 4: rating 2.0. Tastes like chalk Taste is CHALKY - my toddler took one drink and refused any more. I’m drinking the rest of the case as a result, and I don’t particularly enjoy them. Review 5: rating 5.0. Helped my daughter get better! My 2-year-old daughter was very sick and ended up not eating food for a couple of weeks, which led to weight loss, lack of energy, etc. We ended up having to take her off of dairy temporarily until her gut healed from feeling so ill. After doing some research, I bought this and mixed it with Ripple (both are pea protein based). I truly feel it's what brought her "back" and her appetite and energy slowly but surely improved as well. This saved us! Review 6: rating 5.0. 🤗 We like it very much Review 7: rating 5.0. Picky 5 year old will drink this! I rarely write reviews but feel compelled to share our experience. Hopefully this helps someone who is on the fence about paying so much for something they are unsure whether their child will like or not.

He is very underweight and a ridiculously picky eater, even before he was placed on these restrictions. It’s... Review 8: rating 4.0. Great product My elderly Mom tolerates Kate farms nutrition much better at night as compared to the other brand feeds in her PEG tube. Would have given 5 stars but the price is twice the other brands. Review 9: rating 2.0. My son did not like the taste. I gave this to my son who is very small for his age to try to get him to gain weight. I’ve only opened one box and added only a small amount to the pea milk he already drinks. He noticed a difference in taste right away and refused to finish his drink. Review 10: rating 5.0. Great for kids with digestive problems My grandson likes it better than the pediasure!!
No more vomiting with Kate’s farm!
Thank you for a wonderful product😀 Review 11: rating 4.0. Dont let it sit outside. Its usually okay, some packages seem to have been in a heated place stored because they taste and smells different. Almost spoiled. We ended up having to dump a few to a whole box at times
The company said it should never sit outside in heat. Review 12: rating 2.0. Not what I expected While I love the chocolate komplete that kate farms makes, this vanilla formula tastes aweful. I was using the chocolate one for my son who is ftt and needs more calories in addition to his meals and he loves it. His school won't allow "chocolate milk" at school and he doesn't really eat at school because it's too distracting. I was hoping the vanilla version would be the solution to that problem but we opened up one and it honestly tastes like it went bad. Even I... Review 13: rating 5.0. Autistic grandson I have no idea what it taste like, but my 4 year old grandson loves it. He is autistic and very picky about what he will eat or drink. We had to try several before we found this one. It has been a success. Review 14: rating 5.0. Kate Farms is fantastic Although very expensive, it is worth it because it is the only food my niece can tolerate at the moment due to unknown health issues. My sister (her mother) is given formula for her by the state but it always made her sick. I don't mind helping out and buying her the nutrition she needs since I am in a better situation than them and can afford it. Review 15: rating 5.0. Helpful with weight issue of kid with allergy It helps with weight gain and good with taste. Review 16: rating 3.0. Bad reflux Thin milk, needed to thicken a bunch and our two year old that can't eat solids had bad reflux with this Review 17: rating 5.0. Expensive but good! I think this formula is better than enfamil. I wish I knew about it sooner. Yes it’s a little chalky but Abby doesn’t mind. I placed both bottles next to each other and she picked Kate farms. I keep it refrigerated. Review 18: rating 5.0. a must drink for any preemiee baby I love what this company and product stands for, however sometimes the warehouse pulls the purple label over the green label that my twins needs. But this is a must product to ensure overall nutrition for any baby Review 19: rating 5.0. High quality product My kid loves this formula and has improved with hunger control, digestion and BM. I recommend this highly. Review 20: rating 5.0. Great! My daughter was switched to this formula and so far so good. Very fast shipping!
persona: This customer prioritizes functional, high-quality products that solve specific challenges, especially in caregiving or sensitive situations, and is willing to pay a premium for reliability, safety, and positive outcomes, even if the product is slightly more expensive or has minor imperfections.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Life saver for very ill baby I am a medical foster home and I have a little girl that has major health problems due to mom's drug use, she is blind, deaf, has CP, heart problems, seizures, and can not swallow so she must use a G-tube to eat. She was fine on formula but now, at 14 months old her GI Doctor feels she needs to be on something more appropriate for her age and nutritional needs,  we have tried 3 other supplements but she was unable to tolerate them, Kate Farms is the only one that she can keep down. She can not spit up as she asperates which would cause problems that we may not be able to turn around. Thank you for a quick delivery

Review 1: rating 3.0. Nice idea but poor execution Nice idea but poor execution. Babies legs get stuck in. Not easy to put baby in or out. Feet rattles are useless. Cute seat and inliketaht it has back support unlike the Bumbo but not well designed and even harder to take apart. Review 2: rating 5.0. THIS SEAT IS THE BEST!!! This is the best piece of equipment I own for my 5 month old. She's too young for a high chair so this is perfect for her. She loves being in it. We put it right on top of the kitchen table. It's durable, light weight, and fabric is machine washable. I highly recommend this for babies ages 3 mos until they can sit up on their own, and even after.. !! Review 3: rating 5.0. Loved it! This thing! I loved it! Pretty much saved me until my baby grew out of it! I used it when I needed to jump in the shower or do the dishes. My little guy had separation anxiety so I pretty much had to pack him around the house and this little monkey saved my sanity! It's comfy and it has cute little toys attached, which my fella loved. I knew he was safe in it, there was no way he would fall out or over. The only thing is, the little squeekers by the feet are in a funny spot... Review 4: rating 5.0. I put my kid when he still cannot sit alone ... I put my kid when he still cannot sit alone...he denied it. This chair is not used for training him sit but once he can sit, he can sit in the chair playing the included toy for 10-20 minutes. I can have a meal!!! Review 5: rating 3.0. It was not the best fit for our needs It was not the best fit for our needs. It is hard to get the infant in and out of the chair. It is suitable for first stage of sitting when the baby is still relatively small size. Review 6: rating 5.0. My son loves it. He is 2 months old ... My son loves it. He is 2 months old. Wish it had different toys on it but he loves sitting in it Review 7: rating 5.0. This is awesome! Folds flat This is awesome! Folds flat, which is so smart! I wish more baby companies could come up with a way to make there stuff more compact. We used this for many purposes. Even took it with us on a trip once and used it as a "high chair". Review 8: rating 5.0. WONDERFUL Best best best invention ever. I had my 2 month old twins with limited neck control sitting up in this chair checking out the world. It also helps to build neck control. SO much more comfortable for them than the Bumbo even once they have good neck control. I have suggested this chair to SO many moms!! Review 9: rating 5.0. She loves it! I was looking for something besides her bouncer and tummy time to place her while I work in the kitchen. This is perfect now that she is 2 months old, and the price was right. It was simple to assemble, as well. Review 10: rating 5.0. I bought a 2nd one! This product is great! My daughter is just now 3 months old, and still can't sit up on her own. In this seat, she can sit up and look around. I liked it so much, that I bought a second one to take to her day care! Review 11: rating 1.0. One Star Tight squeeze for a boy baby. Review 12: rating 1.0. Don't love it probably shouldn't have purchased this Don't love it probably shouldn't have purchased this. He'll sit in it for up to 15 min at a time. Once he's a bit older I'm sure enjoy the toys more. It's nice and soft and supports well. My only complaint is that there's no way he can squeak the squeakers in the monkey feet. They're in a really weird place and they are more under his legs than under his feet. Review 14: rating 5.0. My 4 month old loves this chair My saving grace! My son cannot yet sit up on his own but refuses to recline. Basically he makes me hold him upright all day. This chair makes us both happy. He gets to sit upright like a big boy and my arms get a break! Review 15: rating 3.0. Tiny As other reviewers have noted this seat is small!! More a floppy piece of fabric that is just for decoration. Review 17: rating 5.0. Perfect for my 9 weeks old boy Worth paying extra. Bought it for my 9-week-old boy since his neck was pretty strong and he started rolling over at 8 week olds. He stayed in it a couple of time a day (happily). He was always happy. Review 18: rating 5.0. The seat is easy to get in and out of and is very ... My 2 1/2 month old granddaughter loves to sit in the seat and watch her big sister play. The seat is easy to get in and out of and is very stable. It folds easily for storage. SUCH a better buy than a Bumbo seat SUCH a better buy than a Bumbo seat! Both of my kids NEVER could manage to get any use out of the bumbo; they were either too little for it, or too big/strong.

persona: This customer prioritizes ease of use and practicality in baby products, especially during solo parenting moments, and is likely to avoid purchases that require assistance or cause distress for the child, favoring items that are intuitive and straightforward to install and use.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Works great for bunk beds Note: For some reason Amazon is listing this product in the children's toys/games category, which it's clearly not. Anyway, moving on...

We needed a bed rail for our toddler, who just moved into a bunk bed. Since the mattress is set in to the frame, most folding guard rails won't work -- the lip of the bed frame would interfere with them. Plus, our bunk bed has slats under the mattress, so side rails with "arms" under the mattress wouldn't have a ... Review 2: rating 5.0. Great for tall matress This item was purchased for our queen size bed so my son could sleep with us. It works perfect. Strong and secure. The height is great but it isn't extra long which is what I was looking for. It is the perfect dimensions for what we needed. Highly recommend. Review 3: rating 5.0. Peace of mind Just switched my 2 yr old granddaughter to a 'big girl bed'. She wiggles around quite a bit in her sleep so was concerned about her falling out. This bedrail performs just as it is supposed to. I don't have any worries now that she will fall to the floor during the night or naptime. It took a bit of wedging in to make it fit inside the bedframe (she sleeps on a daybed). Considered giving it 4 stars because of this but it's not the bedrails fault. Just be forewarne... Review 4: rating 5.0. Nice Bed Rail I needed this bed rail to provide a safe place for my three year old grandson to sleep when he visits. It is on the top of a trundle bed, which has a regular mattress and wire springs. The rail fits perfectly and is snug. It took two people to install it initially, but there is no problem with changing the sheets and the bedding can be tucked down the side between the rail and the mattress. My daughter uses the same rail on a regular bed and she likes it, too. Review 5: rating 5.0. Perfect for platform bed! Since my daughter has a platform bed it's difficult to find a bedrail that fits, this bed rail is perfect, it comes fully assembled and fits snug between the mattress and bed. Review 6: rating 3.0. So, I returned it and I went to Amazon like I should have done in the first place.

This bed rail, although not perfect, is certainly good enough to fit the bill. Review 1: rating 1.0. Dresser was awful/Customer Service worse We purchased the Delta dresser, hoping to use it in our baby nursery. It was packaged very well (lots of bubble wrap and styrafoam), so it made us believe that it may have been broken before being shipped. I called customer service on four different occassions, hoping to get the... Review 2: rating 3.0. Decent quality but not all solid wood We have assembled the dresser today. It took us about 2.5 -3 hours. At least 30 minutes were wasted because one of the grooves in the drawer face-plate was uneven, and this did not allow the drawer bottom and sides to be fit together squarely. So we whipped out some coarse sanding paper and sanded the inside of the groove down, to allow for a better fit.
Other than that there were no major problems with assembly. The piece arrived well-p... reference_output: rating 4.0. Extremely sturdy I'm very impressed with how sturdy this dresser is. It's very heavy and very solid. The direction are extremely clear and the hardware is very well seperated and labeled. Took me about two hours to put together solo and very pregnant. However, it did come with several scratches on the top and one side. It was very well packaged inside the box, even though the box was destroyed, so it seems to me that these scratches were already on the product before it was ever shipped. It's not worth the pain of sending it back, but that's why it only got 4/5 stars. We are very happy with the dresser.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Excellent quality, fits perfectly and were absolute lifesavers as ... I was hesitant as it was very difficult for me to find containers to fit my spectra. There was only 1 review at the time, but I took a chance and purchased. Excellent quality, fits perfectly and were absolute lifesavers as I went back to work and had to pump. Great purchase!

Review 2: rating 5.0. Works great with Spectra pump Showed up a day early :-). Works great with Spectra pump.

Review 3: rating 5.0. Perfect bottles for Spectra! I absolutely LOVE these bottles.  For those who have the Spectra pump know that it only comes with two bottles. After pumping and wanting to store the milk in the fridge right away, I would usually leave it in the Spectra bottles. Then if I wanted to pump again I needed the Spectra ones so I'd have to transfer milk, wash them, dry them and it would be a whole process. I bought the 9oz ones a few days after my son was born. They are good to store (combine)...

Review 4: rating 5.0. Works great. Wish it held a little more Have a spectra s2. Works great. Wish it held a little more, but really, no complaints.

However today I noticed bacteria/mold growing in the silicone lid liner of a few (not all) of the lids. Not safe or healthy. Going into the garbage!

Review 6: rating 5.0. Great collection bottles. I bought these wide-neck breastmilk collection bottles to go along with my Spectra S2 breast pump as my pump only came with 2 bottles. These bottles came in a nice box and partially in plastic.  These bottles fit perfectly on my pump attachment and are solidly constructed.  Probably my favorite feature of this set is the lids.  So many breastmilk collection lids have two components which I am sure have a purpose(nipple to make it bottle for one), but for wha...

Review 7: rating 5.0. fits spectra 2 machine perfectly, and my phillips avent ... fits spectra 2 machine perfectly, and my phillips avent natural bottles. Lids fit so much tighter than the collection bottles that came with my pump.

Review 8: rating 2.0. Falling Apart The seals on 3 of my bottles have fallen out of the lids.  You can pop them back in, but once they're out they come out with each wash.  Very annoying to have to fish the seals out of the sink and put it all back together again.  They are pretty worthless for transporting milk home from work without the seals, so I'll keep fishing them out.

Review 9: rating 5.0. Works with Spectra S1 breast pump. These work perfectly with the Spectra S1 pump; they are interchangeable with the collection kit that came with my pump which is what I was looking for.

Review 10: rating 5.0. good, affordable storage bottles to replace Avent or Spectra wide mouth I use these bottles with my Spectra S2 pump.  The non-cylindrical shape makes them easy to grip when wet, which is convenient when removing the tops in the sink.  The tops seem to seal tight, and I have been using them in the freezer. The scale on the side of oz/ml is easy to read.

Review 11: rating 5.0. Work perfectly with Spectra Pump. These bottles work perfectly with the Spectra II Pump. I have been using them for close to a year now with absolutely no issues. Great price and great product!

Allows me to consolidate and clean once home. So far, lids fit well with no leakage. Bottles seem to be surviving on top rack of dishwasher too. Overall, good product and I will order more as needed.

But even with the seal in there the lid is not sealed whatsoever. It leaks. I have lost a lot of breastmilk to these cheap...

reference_output: rating 4.0. Great bottles for Spectra pump! I got this for my Spectra pump since it only came with 2 and I was sick of constantly having to clean them.  These work perfectly, my only feedback would be that it doesn't show measurements to a full 5 oz (only to something like 4.75) even though the bottle could definitely hold 5 oz, and the measurements are only on the bottle once, so it can be difficult to see how much I've pumped while I'm still pumping. Overall, great set of extra bottles for a good price!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Baby earmuffs I bought these for my sister-in-laws baby shower. I was hoping for the baby to use them less than 3mon of age, which is the suggested age to begin wearing them. They will be perfect since they shoot guns, etc. However I think they will work just fine in October at our clay pidgeon shoot! The ear pieces and head band are soft leather material, and are adjustable to many lengths. Review 2: rating 4.0. Very Happy :) My husband and I purchased this product so that we could take our 6 week old son to a college football game (my husband is a die hard fan and we have season tickets) He was able to sleep through most of the game and there were rarely any instances where we noticed that a noise startled him! We were very impressed :) The only reason that I am not giving these 5 stars is because I found that they were a little difficult to keep on his head ... true he WAS only 6 weeks bu... Review 3: rating 5.0. Great hearing protection for baby Our 9 month old wore the earmuffs for an entire football game. The home team won so it was loud, but she was still able to take her nap Review 4: rating 5.0. Great for Flying Bought this product because I'm a music fan and will love to take my little boy to concerts. I used it for our first trip to the beach and was an excellent help with noise in the airplane. He slept the entire flight. Looking forward to use it in the next trip. Review 5: rating 5.0. Sons loves them! Our son has been able to nap when we've taken him to Disneyland and now on his first plane trip, thanks to these headphones. One man who was sitting behind us on the plane was wondering what magical sounds we were playing on the headphones that enabled our son to be so content and well-behaved. When we told him they were just for noise reduction he seemed very impressed. Your plane neighbors will thank you! Review 6: rating 5.0. muffs These are good muffs for your baby! I use ours so my 5 mo son can be out while we are shooting and firing our cannon. He would not be able to be around all this without the hearing protection with them on he is as happy as can be. Review 7: rating 5.0. Great product, let's us enjoy NBA and College Hoops Our primary use case is sporting events NBA, College Hoops on a biweekly basis. We have used since our baby was 1mo. He is 4mo at the time of this review.

There is some concern about putting these on a baby under 3mo due to their soft skull. While this product cannot official recommend putting it on a baby under this age, we found the tension to be very light. One can reduce it further by stretching out the headband o... Review 8: rating 5.0. Perfect for our LIttle Hockey Fan We have used these twice at live hockey games, and will continue to use them. I'm sure that with out these ear goggles our little 6 month old would have been very upset during the game. He even falls asleep in them, so I know they are comfortable. They expand enough that they fit comfortably on my head too, so I'm not worried that he'll grow out of them anytime soon. Highly recommend! Review 9: rating 5.0. Great! I bought these for my 13 month old son to wear to a skeet shoot. At first he wouldn't wear them but we worked with him for a couple weeks and now he loves them and pulls them out for us to put on him! My son had never been around gunfire and first thing at the skeet shoot was firing off a cannon!! I put the muffs on him and he didn't even flinch! (but I did!) Great quality product! Definitely recommend! Review 10: rating 5.0. Great! I bought this for my infant son because we were attending a fireworks show post-baseball game when he was 3 months old. They worked really well for that occasion and for several other events with loud music.

After hearing how much damage can be caused to infant's ears, I definitely would recommend using these for any event that has a constant loud noise. Also, they are completely adorable. Review 11: rating 5.0. Great protection for baby's ears We bought these in preparatation for the 4th of July and our baby boy. We haven't been able to test them on him yet, but they fit him perfectly and actually expand enough to fit our own heads. We tried them on and they do seem to block sounds out very well. I would recommend this product. Review 12: rating 4.0. Not bad... They are very inexpensive, relatively well constructed, and a decent level of protection. Well padded for highest level of comfort relatively wide range of adjust-ability. Review 13: rating 5.0. Worked Perfectly Bought these for my little one (16 weeks) for when we go target shooting at our personal outdoor range- they worked amazingly well! The noise did't seem to bother him at all. I tried them on myself and they seemed to suppress the noise very well. I highly recommend! (I won't be missing any more concerts either!) Review 14: rating 5.0. Great for 18 month old Purchased to take our 18 month old to a concert. They were comfortable enough for him to keep on for long periods of time, and seemed to cancel out enough noise. Perfect fit on a small setting, and will grow with him. We did not have to use the smallest setting, so this could work for a younger child, too. Review 15: rating 5.0. This made our international flight (>12h) so much better. Baby (5m) slept peacefully through some mild turbulence etc. Also other children and or babies did not wake him up. On an earlier flight one baby would start crying making another baby cry, then another... Review 16: rating 1.0. Broke the second time we used them We took our daughter to two football games. The second time we went to put them on her the plastic connector on the ear broke. Review 17: rating 5.0. Perfect Product I LOVE simple products that do exactly what they need to do without adding a bunch of gimmicky junk. This is one of those products. We bought them because we were heading to Jazzfest with our newborn last spring and they were perfect. We never got too close to the music, but even from a distance it can be tough on a baby's ears, but not with these. She didn't mind wearing them at all and as a bonus, virtually everyone who saw her commented on how cute they looked. Review 18: rating 5.0. Perfect! These earmuffs fit our 7 month old son perfectly. He doesn't mind them being on at all. We go to a lot of loud events (snowmobiles, loud trucks etc) and he wears them without any issues. He hates wearing hats but will wear these without complaint. Review 19: rating 5.0. Rodeo, car show, mowing lawn, and home renovation We bought this when our son was 9 months old for a fair and rodeo. We were in the stands and it was noisy, something that would usually bother him but he wasn't bothered with these on. He sat on grandpas lap the entire time watching the rodeo, just fascinated. Afterwards we walked around the fair, I kept them on him since the fair was noisy. He never pulled them off. I like how they are padded on the ears as well as the portion along ... reference_output: rating 4.0. well made, but baby takes them off, depending on activities... We ordered this for my daughter when she was 7.5 months old for her to wear when we flew to the Mainland from Hawaii.  I used it at home & she slept through me vacuuming without fuss, but on the plane kept taking these off.  They fit well and block sound well.  I still use them (she's 10 months old now) when I wear her in her baby bjorn carrier & vacuum.  For some reason, she doesn't take them off then.  Great product - not sure how to prevent kids from taking the off if they don't want to wear them.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Love this sunscreen! I purchased this sunscreen stick along with the regular Adorable Baby sunscreen so that I could use the stick on my baby's face. If you are a concerned mother like I am about what we are actually putti... Review 2: rating 3.0. Don't Let this Product Get Hot! The next time I used it, however, I was extremely disappointed. It had basically melted, becoming completely useless as a roll-on stick sunscreen. Review 5: rating 5.0. Creamy, light, not white on skin A lot of bad reviews for sunscreens (that I've seen recently comparison shopping) complain about the white look...so based on that, this sunscreen is definitely a hit...it only looks white if you forget to rub it in...which I've done a few times! Rubbing it in is very easy. I am usually applying in the car (not while driving!) and it doesn't take much effort. One reviewer complained about crumbling, but it does not crumble much and obviously if you pu... Review 6: rating 5.0. Truly safe and effective After doing a ton of research, I got this for all of my female family members who go to nail salons and use the UV dryers. It works immediately with no wait time to be effective, provides all of the protection you'd need and is small and compact to fit in purses. It of course works well for babies too! It rates a 1 (which is good) on the EWG's Skin Deep Cosmetics Database, so it doesn't contain dangerous toxins or carcinogens. Great product, highly recommend. Review 7: rating 3.0. We use the lotion version but got this stick for travel use. Review 9: rating 5.0. Must have for your child of any age! I like it because it has a high concentration of zinc oxide...24.7% I have age spots that I'm trying to lighten, so I have to wear a broad spectrum sunscreen. If you have long hair like me...be prepared to have strands of hair stuck on your face... Review 12: rating 4.0. We did not mind the greasiness - it's really only greasy when you first apply, and even then it's far less greasy than liquid sunscreens - and we enjoy the smell of i... Review 13: rating 2.0. The scent is very sweet and not the best Sticky. The scent is very sweet and not the best. Review 14: rating 5.0. Low scoring (good!) in the EWG database Great sunscreen, has protected my baby (8 month old) during short exposures (30 min) at water park and walks. It is easy to use and has a pleasant smell. I looked for its rating in the EWG Skin Deep Database and it scores really low. Review 15: rating 5.0. Easy and Long-lasting At first, I thought it was pricey. persona: This customer prioritizes ease of use, reliable performance, and simple design in products for newborn care, favoring intuitive functionality and low maintenance without unnecessary complexity.
reference_output:
rating 5.0. Love this handy device! This is one of my most used items as a new(er) mother. I have only ever used bulb suction to clean out my LO’s mouth and can’t imagine trying to do that for her nose! She absolutely doesn’t mind the machine and actually laughs when I take it out. Perhaps it tickles a little?
I wash the plastic parts by soaking in warm water and vinegar. The machine holds its charge very well. I like that there aren’t a million buttons to press to adjust suction power like some other models.
Either way, I would recommend this product 100 times over. It works and sells at a more than reasonable price!

Review 1: rating 1.0. Dry!! I have been ordering the huggies in the same packaging but amazon ran out and thought i would give these a try. The wipes were nice and thick but super dry! My daughter did not like them at all.The old formula was better. Please bring it back!! Review 2: rating 5.0. Love huggies! I love these huggies wipes! They are thick enough to where you dont have to use 5 at a time & they are still gentle on the skin! I wont use anything other than huggies wipes. Review 3: rating 2.0. Work, but I hate the texture I have always used Pampers wipes, but decided to try these for something new. I really like the packaging, with the clippy clasp on the top, but I really dislike the feeling of the wipes. They feel like wet paper towels, whereas the Pampers wipes are softer and more fabric-feeling.

They do get my toddler son clean, don't rip, and don't irritate my boy's sensitive skin, but overall I will not be repurchasing. Review 4: rating 5.0. Great for Kittens I had been going through wipes like crazy for the kittens I am raising.

When you have 3 furrballs in litter training, you know how messy it can be.

I didn't see the point in getting the ones at the pet store and running out every 5 mins.

So, I got a pack of 8 at 56-count and they work wonders on the babies.

No fragrance and gets the job done! Review 5: rating 5.0. Worth the extra $ I LOVE thick wipes because these get the job done quickly! It's actually true, they magically can get the job done in one wipe, so you end up saving $ long term. They have a nice scent which I like as well. I also carry them in my car for random clean ups. Nice wipes! Review 6: rating 5.0. Great for wiping dog paws I got these for cleaning my dog's paws, when we come home from outside. It's strong and just moist enough to wipe him clean. I'll go back to the Target store brand wipes after I work through these. Review 9: rating 5.0. Poop Catchers! persona: This customer prioritizes practical functionality, safety, and ease of use in products, often selecting items that solve specific, real-world problems like protection from weather, improved stroller utility, or simplified feeding—while being willing to overlook minor flaws if the core benefits are strong and the product delivers reliable performance in key areas.
reference_output: rating 2.0. Best for uses other than diaper changes We were looking for some wipes for our diaper bag and decided to give these a try.  We did not think that they were a good purchase.  The wipes are definitely thicker, but that didn't reduce the number we needed to use to clean baby's bottom.  As a result, the thickness wasn't actually helpful.  They did seem good with baby's skin, but so do a wide range of other wipes we've tried.  If you're looking for something for something to clean baby's bottom, I wouldn't recommend this.  I think it would be better to carry to clean an older child's hands or face, when the thickness would be more beneficial.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Makes traveling with convertible car seat much easier We balked at the price of this device for a long time and now wish we had bought it immediately upon moving our son to a convertible car seat. It has made traveling with that monstrosity so much easier. Both sets of parents live in other states, so we travel at least twice (usually more) per year. Our Graco Size-4-Me 64 is heavy and has no handles. It's a nightmare to pack through the airport and because of that, we usually check it at bag drop. The Brica Smart Move makes it so much easier to maneuver through with checked bags, back packs, and a 3-year-old, especially if I'm traveling alone with him.

It unfolds in a snap, then secures the car seat quickly and easily. It rolls smoothly and evenly. When we reach bag drop, I unsnap the straps quickly, refold the device, and drop it folded into my checked luggage. I then bag the carseat in a red gate check bag with tag. Highly recommend the Brica Smart Move.

The lids can be difficult to unscrew and since the cups are completely smooth it can be tough to get a good grip.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Recommend to all moms! I switched to Eucerin Baby Wash and Shampoo when my daughter's skin started breaking out from J&J. I use Eucerin lotion and thought I'd give a try. She was still a newborn and her skin cleared up immediately - no baby acne, mild cradle cap, the dry skin went away. I don't use this as a shampoo now but do still use it for their body wash. I'm really happy with it and recommend it to all new mom's.

Review 2: rating 5.0. Good wash for babies with eczema After trying a few brands like Aveeno and Johnson&Johnson, we like this wash/shampoo the best for our baby who has eczema. Soaps up quickly so you can quickly spread it around on the constantly moving baby. We like the feel of the soap, where there some moisturizer to it so it doesn’t dry out the skin, but not too much where it feels too heavy.

Review 3: rating 5.0. Finally tear free soap Tear free and gentle on skin.

Review 4: rating 1.0. Description wrong The description says a pack of 3 but only received 1 single bottle.

Review 5: rating 5.0. commented that my face and skin was looking great. I had been using Metronidazole Gel for Rosacea ... I recently had my annual medical physical and my Dr. commented that my face and skin was looking great. I had been using Metronidazole Gel for Rosacea but had not used it for a month. I told him about the Eucerin Baby Wash and Shampoo I was using to wash my face and he accredited that for the improvement of my skin. SWEET.

Review 6: rating 2.0. Wish it was sulfate free As per wikipedia:  "Like other ethoxylates, sodium myreth sulfate may become contaminated with 1,4-dioxane during production,[2] which is considered to be a Group 2B suspect carcinogen by the IARC."

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Fabulous Changing Pad! I wanted a nice thick changing pad and this met all of my expectations and more! I couldn't believe how much more expensive they were at other Baby Stores. This one was so perfect we actually bought a second to have one upstairs and one downstairs. The size is big which we love, even though our little one is pretty little, she is secure with the straps and contoured sides! Great product and even better price!

Review 2: rating 5.0. Excellent 2nd Changing Pad We bought this as a second changing pad to keep downstairs. My son is 2 years old, and we change him on the floor using this pad. I love that it is longer than the pad on his old changing table, since he's too big for his changing table now. I can squeeze our regular changing pad covers on it but it's tight. My son flops down on it without hurting himself because it's so thick and sturdy. It does the job!

Review 3: rating 4.0. Works well, but watch the straps We bought this changing pad for use with a newborn.  As other reviewers noted it is nice and thick, and is a good size that allowed us to continue using it as he grew older.  On the plus side, the raised edges provide a little extra comfort level for the parent, and also help keep anything unwanted from getting off of the pad (if you have a boy).  On the negative side, as another reviewer noted, the straps can scratch the surface underneath the pad.  ...

I don't think this really even needs a pad cover - you can just wipe it off, and it's not rough plastic.  It's rather soft, actually.  I'm happy with this purchase!

I even tried to adjust it a bit, but nothing helped. Had to return this item.

Review 8: rating 5.0. Gets the job done! This pad was a great deal and so far it gets the job done! It fits with all standard changing pad covers and secures the baby nicely.

My only gripe about this model is that it only has one security strap, I have seen others that have two straps.  But this isn't that big of a deal, as the underside of the pad is a non-slip material so it helps to keep it in place.

The measurements provided for this were accurate and it fits my table perfectly.  It is good quality and a good price.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Worth the money This baby carrier is classy looking yet functional. We purchased the additional newborn insert so that it could be used while he was very little (if you baby is close to 10 pounds you probably don’t need the insert). It has multiple neck support positions and a canopy that rolls up into a pocket. The only downside is that it gets hot for the baby in the summer because of the thick material needed to make it structurally supportive. I use this in stores, outside doing chores, and to calm him when he’s being extra fussy.

It’s a shame because I like that it comes in different sizes and the cute animal prints. Wish I could return but I’ve already used 2 of them...

Review 4: rating 5.0. Great They work great. Wish they worked on the Comotomo bottles but they are silicone so I didnt think they would anyways. I just put a label on the bottle top and it's fine. Works on every other bottle and cup though. I labeled both my kids things for daycare. And they liked the cute animals on them.

Review 5: rating 1.0. Labels did not have adhesive to have the protective cover to go over the label after writing on it. Many of the labels did not have the plastic cover that sticks to the label and therefore they were missing the adhesive.  I was only able to use a few of the labels rather than the entire sheet.  I have used labels like this in the past and had no issues but this allotment of labels was disappointing.

Review 6: rating 5.0. Perfect Labels! We’ve been searching for labels that would work well with our bottles - Joovy Boob - which are quite rounded out towards the base of the bottle. Every label we tried puckered, folded funny and quickly lost its durability. We gave these ones a chance and I’m so glad we did. Super cheap, super cute and absolutely perfect for our needs. Planning on buying a pack or two more to have on hand as our little ones grow up.

Review 7: rating 3.0. Cute, easy to use- not permanent Maybe it's the shape of our bottles (4 ounce avent naturals), but there is no good place to put these that allows them to lay flat. After 2 months of use, i noticed mold growing underneath some of the stickers, and had to replace them. We don't run our bottles through the dishwasher or sterilize them, but we do wash them in hot water and heat them up with a steam bottle warmer. I think for the price, it's ok that these are not permanent. Easier than c...

Review 8: rating 5.0. Day care must have These are great been using for a little over a month and I’ve washed and sterilized my baby’s bottles and the stickers haven’t come off or changed color or anything these are awesome

Review 9: rating 5.0. Great for daycare purposes These are great for bottles going to daycare. I use the stickers for the bottle cap just so they can be found easily and pair with bottle again. For the bottle itself I use the silicone rubber band label that I can erase and change the date. These stickers are more of a "permanent" label...at least until you peel them off. There's a clear sticker that goes over what you write, so even after washing it's still properly labeled. I've only used them for a week...

Review 10: rating 5.0. 5 Star Product! These are the best labels I have found. They stick very well to the bottles, the writing does not smudge when u laminate and the design is super cute! I have even put the bottle a through the dishwasher a couple of times and the labels held up. I peeled one off just to test and see if it would leave a residue on the bottle and it didnt, that made me like em even more! They are exactly what they are described to be. Definetly well worth the eight dollars. I highly reco...

Review 11: rating 5.0. AWESOME These labels are just what I needed. They are small but serve the purpose I need, which is to put the feeding time on my son's bottles for daycare. The self-laminating feature works well. I've washed the bottles several times just to test the durability and they are still intact. They are easy to remove and don't leave any residue. Great, great product. I've already recommended them to a friend.

Review 12: rating 2.0. Not adhesive Cute color and design for the price. Labels to not lay flat or stay adhered to all material. I need to replace the label everyday and at the end of the day it looks like this or is gone. Top layer also wrinkles away from main label where the name is written.

Review 15: rating 5.0. Does what it says I ordered these labels to label my daughters Dr. Brown’s bottles for daycare. The labels also did start to come up on each side after the first cycle in the dishwasher, but again, not a deal breake...

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. So incredibly lightweight and perfect for my kids I bought this stroller for a trip we had to San Diego. I have a 3 year old and a 1 year old. It is more lightweight than my single stroller. I knew I'd need a place for my 1 year old to nap when we were on the go and every other sit and stand I found didn't have a reclining front seat, so that was a huge plus for us. Also the sunshade was amazing, so easy to get both kids out of the sun.
Definately recommend! Review 2: rating 4.0. Happy with purchase Glad we purchased a sit and stand since our kids are 3.5 years apart. Has worked out great. Only complaint as a previous bob user is the maneuverability and durability of the wheels. They already look pretty worn and squeak occasionally since we stroll quite a bit on sidewalks and outdoors. Review 3: rating 4.0. I love the stroller I love the stroller... I just don't like that it can't up with the umbrella attachment and the car seat attachment still in place! Other than that, I love it!! Review 4: rating 4.0. Great simple stroller Great simple stroller. Love the sit and stand option for our almost 3 year old and our infant. He gets to walk when he wants, or hop on if he is tired or if we want to move quickly. Perfect for the zoo, or locations like that. If I lived in the city I would probably get a proper double, but since we live in the suburbs and only use this a couple times a week for walks to the park/ or when we walk our dog all together it works great. Handles well and is not to h... Review 5: rating 2.0. not so easy to drive in bumpy roads Very big, not so easy to drive in bumpy roads Review 6: rating 5.0. Very compact and convenient Excellent compact stroller for travel and in vehicle uses. We almost prefer using this over our $2000 Bugaboo Donkey. The standing feature is really cool, although our 3 year old still prefers to sit. Review 7: rating 5.0. Absolutely LOVE it! I bought mine sometime last year and loved it; my niece needed a double stroller when she had her fourth child a couple of months ago, so I bought her one too! I was disheartened to learn that it was much cheaper for hers than I paid for mine, but it is what it is. It is a fantastic, light (compared to all other double/tandem strollers) and easy open/close. LOVE LOVE LOVE IT! Review 8: rating 4.0. Good Product I bought the Joovy from Amazon because it was about US$100 cheaper than in my own country. It was shipped to my relative's US address and he checked it in as luggage when he flew back home. As such, I was very concerned about warranty. Despite its website link on warranty, Amazon could not help at all and offered a credit into my account after asking me to contact Joovy directly.
Sales experience aside, I usually run errands on my own with both children (1 and 4 yea... Review 9: rating 5.0. Our Go-To Double I was having such a hard time deciding on a double stroller. This is my every day, keep in the car, easiest for travel, go to choice!

I have a used phil & teds jogger that I have only used as a double a handful of times, but am keeping to have the option to jog with one or two kids. It is fairly light and easy to maneuver, but doesn't feel very sturdy, doesn't have a good shade, doesn't have much storage (or any on the handle), and doesn't fold well.
... Review 10: rating 5.0. Very pleased I purchased the Joovy Caboose Ultralight Graphite for use with my almost-three-year-old son and infant daughter. I was sure my son would want to hop on and off as he pleased when we go on walks. To my surprise, he preferred to ride seated on the bench! As he now often rides his Strider or tricycle, I am happy that the stroller is a reasonable size in comparison to a single stroller.

I read a ridiculous number of reviews before finally purchasing. After a few m... Review 11: rating 1.0. My daughter does like the backseat but it is such a pain to ... Not impressed by this stroller. It is a struggle to make turns, not what I expected. My daughter does like the backseat but it is such a pain to make easy turns with this stroller Review 12: rating 3.0. I kick into the back of the stroller... Overall, this stroller is pretty nice. We use the Bumbleride Indie Twin when we're home, however, we spend quite a bit of time at our cabin throughout the year, and the Bumbleride is WAY too big to cart back and forth. We were looking for a reasonably priced, second stroller to leave at the cabin. My main use for it there is to take our girls (ages 5, 3, 1 yrs old) on walks, and the occasional vacation-type activities that require a stroller. ... Review 13: rating 1.0. Feels Like Pushing a Grocery Cart I bought this stroller hoping it would work for my newborn and toddler. It doesn't. It's way too long and that makes it awkward to steer and take anywhere. Forget anything other than a smooth tiled floor. The sidewalk is basically impossible since every tiny bump feels basically stops the stroller dead in its tracks. Steering on a sidewalk is also very challenging because any slight slant or dip in the sidewalk and the stroller will lean all the way ... Review 14: rating 3.0. Expected more I read so many amazing reviews on this stroller and decided to go for it. I loved that it was lightweight and it didn't look too big. I was a little disappointed though. I didn't think it was light and it looks a lot bigger in person, also when folded it is huge. It is such a hassle to take out of the car and then to put it in that I rather put my 2 month old in a carrier and have my 3 year old walk. I was really hoping I would love it. Review 15: rating 3.0. Expected more for my money Pros:
Lightweight
Maneuvers easily - handles turns very well.
Canopy is huge and provides great coverage not only to the front passenger, but rear passenger as well.
Seats are firm and provide a very comfortable ride for children.

Cons:
Requires two hands to close, and doesn't stand on its own when closed.
If you want to use an infant seat, you must remove the snack tray and insert a special attachment for the infant... Review 16: rating 5.0. Love it! Perfect for infant and toddler One of the best double stroller options out there. Universal carseat adapter let's you put baby up front in the carseat, while your other little one rides in the back. If your toddler is too young to use the sit and stand option, the rear seat added on is a great option. Easy assembly, lightweight and compact. Same length front to back as our city mini. I'm in love! Review 17: rating 5.0. Finally! The Holy Grail of everyday strollers for two tots Officially my favorite everyday stroller!
This is my 5th stroller, after two babies- now two toddlers (one that likes to be independent and walk around. It gets tired after a while) I really needed something light, easy to pick up by myself, get in/out of the trunk and push/maneuver with one hand. This Joovy Ultralight does it all! My 2.5 year old loves to sit, stand and jump on/off the back ledge. I love the buckles for... Review 18: rating 5.0. Double stroller We have been using this with my newborn and 2.5 yr old and so far so good! My husband and I like the size and easy manueverability of this stroller. My older daughter likes the option of sit/stand. She is a very active 2.5 yr old and most of the time walks everywhere so she only uses the stroller for a few mins if she wants to take a break. So far it fits our needs perfectly. Review 19: rating 3.0. Three Stars It does not come with the infant car seat strap even doe the instruction says it does Review 20: rating 5.0. Their 4 year old can stand up in the back while the 2 year old sits - they love it. It makes it so easy Bought this for our daughter and her husband. Their 4 year old can stand up in the back while the 2 year old sits - they love it. It makes it so easy!
persona: This customer values practicality and multi-tasking functionality in strollers, making purchase decisions based on real-life usability and long-term convenience, and is willing to invest in a product that simplifies complex, high-load situations despite initial hesitation over cost.
reference_output: rating 5.0. This stroller is worth the money I have contemplated buying this stroller for about 6 months, I held off because I figured I could push my 2 yr old's stroller and just wear the baby in a carrier. Until a few days ago, when I was pushing the baby in the stroller, carrying the toddler, as well as a huge swim ring and a stupid zebra push toy and I decided it was enough. So i bought the joovy and boy it has already been a lifechanger! It doesn't seem to be any heavier than my single stroller (GB Evoq) and is easy to push. It holds the baby's carseat (GB asana) perfectly! And I love the slim profile, although when the front seat is reclined, it really takes away space from the backseat. It's ok though because I would rather have the lightness of the stroller and the ease of use, so it's a fair trade off. I need  to buy the back seat add on, because my 2 yr old just isn't quite balanced enough to ride on the back seat board just yet.

Review 6: rating 1.0. These cups have weird cup, after I gave it ... These cups have weird cup, after I gave it to my boy. He couldn't get the juice out. So I then tried it myself I got soapy water. I've let the kid air dry for two days after wash and the soap water still inside. Can't be rinsed away. Parents please try it yourself before give it to your baby. Review 7: rating 5.0. Five Stars Does a good job of keeping the liquid from spilling. Review 8: rating 5.0. replaceable valves--Hallelujah! 5 stars for three reasons:
1) it doesn't spill
2) IT HAS REPLACEABLE VALVES!!! The valve is also only one piece, not two that you need to take apart for cleaning (which doubles the chance you're going to lose it). It bothers me so much that most cups are useless if you lose or damage the valve. It's like the designers of most kids' toys/eating products/etc just straight up don't care about the environment.
3) It is a nice size; not too s... Review 9: rating 3.0. Great cups These cups are great. They're easy to hold, easy to clean and do not leak. Easy To Clean, Lightweight, & Durable I like these sippy cups a lot because they're easy to clean. (There's a solid white plastic piece that attaches to the lid as opposed to the typical, rubbery attachment that you see on a lot of sippy cups). Very large, yet lightweight. A lot of the 10 oz sippy cups you see on the market are heavy. They're a little wide, so I'm not sure how beginners would do with these cups. My daughter is 18 months and she has no problem at all. I would de... Review 11: rating 3.0. Too hard to drink from My little girl was 16 months old when we started using these. They definitely don't leak, but they are just a bit too hard for my little one to get a good drink from. It made it so she just wouldn't drink and in turn became constipated and dehydrated. I kept trying different cups and finally found one of the nuby straw cups to work best. Review 12: rating 5.0. Wouldn't buy any other sippy Best sippy cups ever. They don't leak. They're easy to clean. Daughter loves them. What more can I ask? These are the only sippy's I ever buy now. Review 13: rating 4.0. Does not leak! I've had my 2 year old and 1 year old girls using these sippy cups for the past 2 weeks. I have not seen one leak, not even once! Even when held upside down, not one drip! My daughters do not have any problem drinking from them. The spouts are hard and they don't have to bite down or anything like with some no-leak cups.

They only reason I don't give these cups 5 stars is because the hourglass shape makes them inconvenient for travel. They do not fit in... Review 14: rating 4.0. Decent cups The biggest "pro" about these cups is perhaps the biggest "con". The narrower center portion of the cup is great for enabling our baby to hold on to it easily BUT it also means that when he's being a little lazy and doesn't tip the cup completely vertical, there's an ounce or so of fluid that remains in the bottom.

A problem we have encountered with one of the two cups we received is that the lid is very difficult to get on to the bottle squarely, especially w... Review 15: rating 5.0. Works great. No leaks This product does exactly what it is supposed to do. No leaks.

However, I bought a few more last night at Walmart for $2.50 each. (03/06/11)

FANTASTIC CUPS. Daughter is 18 months. The least amount of spillage of any cup I have seen!!!! And my daughter TRIES!!!! Easy for her to hold being a larger size cup!

The individual vials are very convenient, and each vial is labeled with the product name. I keep a strip of them in the diaper bag, beside her crib and keep the rest in ... Review 12: rating 2.0. I am pleased with it as it really helps! And my daughter likes the taste so much she takes it without a problem.
we tried "regular" meds and they just didn't help. Thankful to find a product that does.
I started buying off Amazon since the price is not much more for a bigger box.
I just wish I could buy it in a bottle and use a dropper to dispense, rather than the little plastic. Doses. Review 14: rating 4.0. Provides relief! They are easy to use and I love that they are safe and homeopathic! Review 15: rating 1.0. not impressed This didn't work for my teething baby at all. reference_output: rating 3.0. Teething drops This stuff seemed to work well at first but now that my son is older I think it doesn't work as well. My son started teething at 2 months and I started giving this to him at about 3 1/2 months. The taste is just fine with him, he doesn't spit it out like he does with most liquids. In my opinion it is worth a try, especially if your baby is anything like mine and has a miserable time with teething. Different things work for different babies. I personally started using the amber teething necklace for my son and it has helped a ton. But like I said this is worth a try.

reference_output: rating 4.0. The scan feature is great - it scans from one room to the other ... The monitor (fully charged) only lasts a few hours tops. I will be buying an additional charger for it so that I have one upstairs and downstairs. reference_output:
rating 5.0. Great product! I'm really happy with this stroller. Performed like a champ with two busy 3 and 4 year olds (35lbs and 47lbs). No issues with steering! 
.I bought the BubuBee XL double stroller travel bag and it was easy to bag and traveled well. Bagged it at the gate and picked it up after arrival. I will otherwise just drive it everywhere.
.It's big, yet it fit through all doors I went thru and maneuvered great even in tight spots. Make sure to unlock the front wheels (They make it hard to maneuver when locked).
.Rain cover sold by Joovy fit well; i installed it and wrapped it under the handle bar to have it setup and ready to go in case of sudden rain (used velcro straps to keep it tight and it caused no issues closing the stroller, nor driving it!
.Cargo is spacious!
.Canopy is big & covered the Florida sun well. Reflectors are bright at night! Nice touch!
.Seats recline almost to horizontal position => made for great naps
.Loved the one hand recline & that each seat reclines individually: 1 child could nap while the other was enjoying; it also gave them privacy
.Kids loved the seat-side pockets: Zippy cups, hats, water, sunglasses, small toys... you name, it they put it all there!
.bumper bar was easy to remove. I used a shoe string to hold it below the handle bar center, above the canopy about 2 inches under the handle bar, when not in use or when bagging the stroller for travel
.Brake bar was easy to engage & disengage (very convenient). I'm 5'8", hubby is 6'2", we did not experience issues hitting the break with feet when walking
.Yes, seats need to be reclined to close the stroller!
.Car seat stands on its side while closed; so do not expect to move it around much when closed.
.The suspension is great and holds tons of weight without issues.
The only issues were size related: 1) loading it into parking trains at Disney => we either had to wait to get the double face-to-face front seats, or we walked to/from the entrance; 2) getting thru Disney's steam train station => took the space of 3 people on train bench, but we managed (and stayed in the train for 1.5 laps to make it worth while); 3) still big when closed: took almost the whole trunk of a jeep encore or escape SUV. I ended up putting 1 luggage piece at the feet of the smaller kid's car seat, 1 in between the 2 car seats, 1 under the stroller, and the carry-on bag on my lap. Apply the breaks after loading the stroller made it possible to close the back trunk door without laboring to keep the stroller propped over the seats; 4) plastic part on either side of center of the handlebar is looked like it was cracking. I put a bit of aluminum tape to make sure the two pieces did not separate and the stroller traveled 6 airplane rides, 3 Disney Parks, Sea World, and strolling around for 5 more vacation days without any problems. The "cracked appearance" did not affect the performance of the stroller at all and the tape I used helped keep those pieces in place.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Excellent quality. Necessity! Perfect for sandy hair after the beach, cradle cap, food smeared on baby's head, you name it! I use these on my baby and my 3 year old boys!! Just like the hospital ones but nicer with the little sponge, etc. I was worried the sponge would trap water and cause these to get moldy and gross faster, but that has not been the case! I've not had any trouble keeping clean! Been using for at least 6 months. Review 2: rating 5.0. Worked well! Great for caring cradle cap! I washed my baby's head with this sponge and Mustela foam shampoo. They worked well for her and cradle cap had gone from her head after about one week since we used them. Review 3: rating 2.0. Two Stars Fell apart in less than 1 week. Review 4: rating 5.0. Helps for Sensory I bought the Scrubbie for my son, who has sensory issues. He loves to have his back, arms, and legs scratched gently and complains about my fingernails being "too rough". I thought the soft teeth of the Scrubbie would help & it does! Plus, he can use it himself. Review 5: rating 5.0. Five Stars These are great! The work wonderfully and help my son get rid of cradle cap. Review 6: rating 5.0. Great. These are exactly what I needed after I lost the sponges they gave me in the hospital, love 'em and so does my daughter! Review 7: rating 5.0. Must for babies. Love these! We received these from the hospital when our first and second were born (10 and 7 years ago). When we had our newest we didn't get these and it took us a bit to figure out what was missing in our bath routine. These scrubbies are awesome and I buy them for baby showers. They work really well with cradle cap and are so gentle. This is almost a must for my babies! Review 8: rating 5.0. Best product for cradle cap! A YouTube Mom recommended these little scrubbies online and it is the best thing ever! Admittedly my daughter had little cradle cap, but what she did have was gone after one use. Even if your child has no cradle cap if they have a lot of hair like my daughter it gives a great clean and thoroughly gets every hair. I was using another baby sponge before this and this sponge is high quality! It holds a lot of suds and is super soft. Review 9: rating 5.0. Review 1: rating 5.0. Lifesaver! LOVE Colic Calm! As a single mom of g/g twins you can imagine my horror of both of them in distress of some kind screaming in gas pain! HUGE Help and results were immediate!!!!
Thank you Colic Calm! Review 2: rating 3.0. Three Stars It worked great for the gas relief, but then it made my baby really constipated. Had to stop. Review 3: rating 4.0. the homeopathic ingredients are great they definitely have their place I bought this for my colic little girl, because of all the reviews and what other people had told me. so far the only thing I notice it changes is calms her down when she really gets going. the homeopathic ingredients are great they definitely have their place. this in my opinion is more of a calming substance. I think it helps, she definitely calms down a bit when I give it to her and she seems to like the taste ... Review 4: rating 3.0. Miracle? We've tried a few different methods (Mylicon, Equate gas drops, Bioron Cocyntal Drops, etc.) with no luck. Colic Calm is NOT cheap by any means, but I was willing to try it due to all the rave reviews.

First, this stuff is black...which is really weird to feed your baby. ... Review 5: rating 5.0. HAVE THIS ON HAND when home from the hospital A godsend during the first 9 colicy weeks. Good Product We have used Colic Calm with our 12 week old since her first or second week. It has helped tremendously with her upset stomach and gas. The syringe is great for medicine delivery. Helped my newborn and me get some sleep. He took this easily and seemed to get instant relief. persona: This customer prioritizes product effectiveness, especially for infant health concerns, and values reliable performance over aesthetics; they are willing to deviate from recommended dosages if results are positive and trust well-constructed, easy-to-assemble items with sturdy build quality.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Best thing ever! Best thing ever! For the first 2 and half weeks of my son's life he stayed miserable and gassy and wasn't sleeping much. We tried gripe water and gas drops from the store but it never worked. We found out he has silent acid reflux so he's been pretty miserable. I tried this colic calm inbetween his medicine and this stuff has worked. I've only been giving him half of the recommended dose and within minutes he's calmed down and sleeping well. We are finally feeling better and catching up on all our missed sleep. I actually tried this myself and didn't find it to be bad at all. My son takes it very well. He doesn't spit up as much because of his silent acid reflux but I still keep a cloth under his chin for the first few minutes so we don't get the stuff on his clothes. I'd recommend this to anyone before I would any other product to relieve gas/colic.
