reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Baby loves them These are really cool.  When the Golden State Warriors went to the finals, we weren't going to miss it.  Our daughter was only 6 months old and we didn't want to leave her.  So we took her and she had such a great time.  She even fell asleep with her headphones on, during some of the loudest parts of the game.

Review 2: rating 5.0. Great for Flying Bought this product because I'm a music fan and will love to take my little boy to concerts. I used it for our first trip to the beach and was an excellent help with noise in the airplane. He slept the entire flight. Looking forward to use it in the next trip.

Review 3: rating 5.0. ... six month old son dove hunting and these worked great!! We took our six month old son dove hunting and these worked great!!

Review 4: rating 2.0. These will not work, just Stand further away from noises They are tight. Our 8mo old had red marks on his ears and he has a small head (25-50th percentile) not a giant water head like his uncle. Still these ear muffs nearly squished his little nugget flat and obviously he hated them and removed them at every possible time he could get his hands free. Worthless.

Review 5: rating 4.0. Blocks sound, puts baby to sleep, but a little big. These work great and put our baby to sleep from time to time.  The only down side is that they are a little too big for babies under 1 year old (we have a girl). They can easily slip off if the baby moves. We use them for church services (our church music is progressive and can be a bit loud) and they do there job but we do find ourselves repositioning them 2-3 times per service.  I think it should marketed for 1-3 years old.

Review 6: rating 5.0. Great hearing protection for baby Our 9 month old wore the earmuffs for an entire football game. The home team won so it was loud, but she was still able to take her nap

Review 7: rating 5.0. Awesome Perfect for my infant so that he and mom can be present while I am shooting.  I Would definitely recommend

Review 8: rating 5.0. Worked Perfectly Bought these for my little one (16 weeks) for when we go target shooting at our personal outdoor range- they worked amazingly well! The noise did't seem to bother him at all. I tried them on myself and they seemed to suppress the noise very well. I highly recommend! (I won't be missing any more concerts either!)

Review 9: rating 5.0. Excellent noise reduction! These fit my 6 month old perfectly for a concert we attended. I placed them over my ears before we went and I was pleased with the sound reduction. Also they fit my 2 and 4 year old sons well too!

Review 10: rating 3.0. baby ear phone Bought these for our boston terrier so she could go Airboating with us  I am having to alter them But I think they will work.

Review 11: rating 5.0. Love it My baby,who is 4 month old now,doesn t like noisy sound:fireworks,loud music ....and this is perfect for him.we can go out with him and the noise doesn t bother him anymore,furthermore he is so cute with this :).we bought it when he was 2 month old

Review 12: rating 5.0. Used for taking an infant on a Single engine plane ... Used for taking an infant on a Single engine plane.  Although he can't tell me he likes it my older kids say it quietens down the cabin noise as much as their standard aviation headsets.

Review 13: rating 1.0. Baby can't stand these Baby refused to wear this. Sending it back!

Review 14: rating 5.0. These worked great at the KINGS STANLEY CUP rally These worked great at the KINGS STANLEY CUP rally...  My newborn was in love with the sound of winning and they work great for SHARKS fans who are constantly choking on everything it really blocks it out......

Review 15: rating 2.0. Works great but the fit is uncomfortable Works great but the fit is uncomfortable.  Baby Boy keeps yanking it off.  Gave it to my sister who has a son with auditory processing disorder to help him drown out sounds.

Review 16: rating 2.0. Two Stars Big and bulky for 5 month old with life jacket.

Review 17: rating 2.0. These seemed necessary at one point, but in handsight aren't functional.  Not recommended. We used these once at a music festival when my son was 3 months old.  They worked fine at the time, though I'm not sure how necessary they were since the festival was outside.  I put them on and didn't get the impression that they block sound well enough to make me feel comfortable taking a baby to an indoor concert.  Once my son hit about 6 months, he wouldn't keep them on anymore. He immediat...

Review 18: rating 4.0. Baby earmuffs I bought these for my sister-in-laws baby shower. I was hoping for the baby to use them less than 3mon of age, which is the suggested age to begin wearing them. They will be perfect since they shoot guns, etc. However I think they will work just fine in October at our clay pidgeon shoot! The ear pieces and head band are soft leather material, and are adjustable to many lengths.

Review 19: rating 5.0. Awesome.. work perfect Awesome..work perfect.  I have a 2 year old son with a big head and they fit him perfectly!

Review 20: rating 5.0. Sons loves them! Our son has been able to nap when we've taken him to Disneyland and now on his first plane trip, thanks to these headphones. One man who was sitting behind us on the plane was wondering what magical sounds we were playing on the headphones that enabled our son to be so content and well-behaved. When we told him they were just for noise reduction he seemed very impressed. Your plane neighbors will thank you!
persona: This customer prioritizes multi-functional, practical products that enhance comfort and convenience in real-life situations, values durable and aesthetically pleasing design, and is willing to invest in items that solve specific sensory or usability challenges for their child.
reference_output: rating 5.0. FAVORITE BABY PRODUCT!!! We absolutely love our Baby Banz! We take them EVERYWHERE! I originally got them for my 2 month old to wear at a college football game. After purchasing them I've discovered many more uses for them including loud venues, festivals, live music, sporting events, etc. Also great for airplanes! They have actually come in handy the most at loud restaurants! Sometimes noisy restaraunts are just over stimulation for a tired baby. As soon as I put on the baby banz he immediately calms down and usually goes to sleep! I carry them in my diaper bag everywhere. They are also my new go-to baby gift for new parents. Such a practical gift that most people don't know about. HIGHLY RECOMMEND!!!!!

I hate to say the "m" word, but, this stuff works like magic. We've used a dozen doses so far, and it's worked every time. We had been using Mommy's Bliss... it helped, but not to the degree that Colic Calm does.. and Colic Calm does it without Sodium Bicarbonate, which can contribute to certain stomach discomforts.
He seems to enjoy the taste and is usually eagerly opening his mouth for the dropper so he can taste it.

reference_output: rating 3.0. changed their product I used to love these wipes.  However, they recently changed them.  Now the wipes are much thicker.  This could be a bonus if you are wiping a poop-covered bottom each time (needing lots of wiping power!).  But we have been following the "Infant Potty Training" method (see Laurie Bouke's wonderful book for details), and our wiping need is very minimal.  I feel like I am wasting a wipe each time -- it is more than we need.  At least I am able to wash them with our other cloth diapers (dry them on the line) and reuse them another time or two!  Still, I was able to do this with the previous wipe just as well!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 3.0. Not sure I had no ambitions of making my own baby food. I bought this to be able to smash the food we normally eat and feed to our baby during dinner but it didn't work well for my purposes. We eat a lot of rice and beans (I'm Puerto Rican) but it didn't work for that. It doesn't smash the rice well enough for me to feed to my 6mo old with confidence that she won't choke. reference_output: rating 5.0. Even grinds beef! I had this product in 1992 for my kids.  It is very hard to find now so get it while you can. reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Great price and comfortable. This mattress fits perfectly in the crib and is comfortable. Soft, but firm for the baby. It has a good price and the material is of excellent quality and comes with a waterproof vinyl cover that is easy to clean. I really liked that! My baby has been sleeping better, napping during the day has been longer. My experience has been good. Review 2: rating 3.0. Not firm enough This mattress is not firm enough like my old one. I wish it was more thicker. But still better than the one I bought from Walmart Review 3: rating 5.0. Just what we needed I really love that this mattress is greenguard certified. As first time parents, we are really trying to provide the best for our baby boy and having a safe, hypoallergenic mattress is a start.

Our baby boy has been sleeping on this new mattress for about a week now. I noticed that he has been sleeping longer in the crib. I love that the mattress is liquid resistant, which will be great as we near the toddler stage and then eventually the potty training... Review 4: rating 4.0. One side board is splintered - but good design One side board is splintered , so crazy glue and piece of vinyl tape, too much work to send back. Over all it's good enough. Review 5: rating 2.0. Not comfortable. We ordered this for our Frozen Toddler bed. It was cut when we took it out of the box. Also, it isn’t comfortable to our 4yr old. She complains that her back hurts when she’s in it. So we don’t make her sleep on it. We will be ordering another brand. Perhaps something a little more durable. Review 6: rating 4.0. Basic Kids Bed This is a good mattress for small children. The waterproof feature is a time saver on those nights our son has accidents. It is easy to clean and not heavy to lift. I would like for this bed to be a bit more comfortable. The construction is just springing underneath and you can feel them when you lay down. It is a firm and basic kid bed. Review 7: rating 1.0. I had to return it because it was so stiff I really wanted to keep this because it seemed to be good quality. Unfortunately my son is 3 now and wouldn’t sleep on it because it was so stiff. Review 8: rating 1.0. Color was not right it was yellowish I ordered a White one ! Came in Yellowish color and not a pure white as advertised!
The yellowish color was in certain areas not throughout the crib ! It was an easy assemble. Review 9: rating 1.0. Don’t waist your money This bed is hard as a rock my daughter said she would rather sleep on the floor Review 10: rating 5.0. Great mattress I bought this mattress for my daughter big girl bed can I say she absolutely loves her bed !!! The mattress is extremely comfortable she has not left her bed since putting it together and it’s all thanks to this incredible mattress!!!! Buy it !!! You’re little people will thank you Review 11: rating 5.0. Soft and fits toddler bed well. Very comfortable for 3 year old. Review 12: rating 2.0. very hard felt like more of a box spring then a mattress Review 13: rating 1.0. Lumpy and pokes you The mattress is uncomfortable to lay on... lumpy and it feels like your being poked.. terrible bed do not buy!!! Review 14: rating 1.0. Mattress is Good It was too big for my bed Review 15: rating 2.0. Hard I don't feel it's that comfortable but my 2 year old doesn't seem to mind it Review 16: rating 2.0. Pretty hard This is a well built mattress but its super hard. I thought it was a dual side mattress, one for infant one for toddler but its not. If it had a bit more cushion it would be great.
persona: This customer values positive emotional experiences and simplicity in their purchases, often responding favorably to products with a cheerful, straightforward appeal, but is notably sensitive to product fit and accuracy in product descriptions, especially when items are bundled or paired.
reference_output: rating 3.0. Misleading! Misleading! Did not fit bed frame that it was paired with (Mini Mouse Frame). Too big, it pushed all side out to the point of bending.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Great Tool It has a lot of detail you can skip, I have twins, so instead of recording feedings, we use the next row to record the second child's sleep patterns so my caretaker and I are "on the same page" as to how they're doing.

Very well designed. Review 2: rating 4.0. Good for NICU babes No need to purchase a baby tracker for home use, but if you need to keep track of babies input and output for medical reasons, this book has the best format. I bought mine because I am very type A, but ended up not using it. Review 3: rating 5.0. Every new parent NEEDS this log book!! This is probably one of the greatest inventions. Before my baby was born, I purchased this and figured it's a nice-to-have item.
I didn't realize until the first day home from the hospital how I would be RELYING on this simple, but easily organized log book. When you are sleep deprived and learning everything for the first time, you don't know which way is up or down sometimes. It can be extremely overwhelming, especially when breastfeeding... Review 4: rating 5.0. Must have for new moms This is the best way to keep track of when your baby eats, sleeps, poops, gets a bath, etc. It's also a great way to keep track of which side baby nursed on. I took this to all infant doc appt. And it's a great gift! Review 5: rating 5.0. Life-saver! Please, please take this to the hospital with you- you'll thank me later. We did, but it stayed in the suitcase for 2 days because we were SO "out of it" dealing with my c-section,, etc. We had been keeping notes -on loose sheets of paper- of baby's feedings and diaper output (so crucial those first few days). We were also using an iPhone app that proved more annoying than useful. Whenever a nurse or pediatrician walked in the room, we couldn't find which paper ... Review 6: rating 5.0. Great product! I have used this book since my son was born. It's a great and handy way to keep track of when and how much eats and when I change his diaper. Makes it easy to discuss his progress with his doctor. This journal is great for his grandparents who watch him while I'm gone. They are able to maintain his schedule and it makes it very easy for me to see how he is doing while I'm at work. Highly recommend this journal for all parents of children especially for those with ... Review 7: rating 5.0. Perfect for nanny We have used this since my nanny started at 6 weeks and now almost 6 months. It's been a very helpful tool to look back and see his patterns. Also helpful when someone else watches him because the way he eats with a bottle is totally different than nursing when I'm home with him. I kept track of my Nancy's hours and his milestones in it. It's a keepsake of his milestones and it will be a part of his baby box. Review 8: rating 5.0. Useful beyond belief So glad I bought these, had twins and remembering anything is next to impossible. There were four of us chipping in around the clock this was a great way to let each other know what has been happening. Review 9: rating 4.0. Really Helpful! This is really helpful, especially since the first day I got for my 5 month old she got really sick and I was able to have the information that I need for the pediatrician. The only thing I don't like is how the times are laid out, but I can deal with that Review 10: rating 2.0. Doesn't work for me I gave this tracker a good try, but couldn't make it work for my breast fed baby. I found the small boxes restrictive and didn't really like having to draw arrows to indicate the times for each category. I don't tend to be a "get baby on a strict schedule" kind of mama, which may be why I didn't like this tracker. I just wanted to track feedings and diapers for the pediatrician visits and to get a general feel for emerging patterns. I do really like th... Review 11: rating 4.0. Could be very useful... if I didn't suck at documenting. Not sure exactly what made me think that I could keep up with recording every feeding, diapering, and sleep session. I was able to keep up with it for about 3 weeks...and then it fell by the wayside. I can really see how this would help with finding a schedule but I decided to let baby lead since we have a very flexible lifestyle. Review 12: rating 5.0. Great Tracker! I've been using this since the day my baby was born (1 month ago) and it is a life saver! It is a great way to track when I breastfeed, what breast he ate from, how long, etc. Plus when pee diapers are changed and poopy diapers. Our Pediatrician is always asking # of Wets and # of Dirty Diapers per day...so I bring this book to our appts and let her see it all. I color code each item so its very easy to tell at a glance when he last ate, slept, or pottied. I plan ... Review 13: rating 5.0. ... compared several journals and this is by far the best layout compared to other baby day tracker journal out ... Searched and compared several journals and this is by far the best layout compared to other baby day tracker journal out in the market. compact, concise, with plenty of space to write and fill with anything. great during baby's 1st 6 months although i didn't keep up and never finished using the journal. Review 14: rating 4.0. Needs some adjustments The over all journal is very nice, I dont use their suggested 'codes' but it works for me. My biggest complaint is the layout of the times. It's very confusing having it go from 6am on 1 page than switch to another day half way through the next page. The journal would be perfect if it just did the left side starting at 12am and finished on the right at 11:30am so when the book is open flat you see the full day. I have to try to remember where I'm at and at 5am ... Review 15: rating 5.0. A must-have for new moms I purchased this right before my little one was born. It was very easy to record everything. The best thing about it was when my hubby or mom would take a baby shift, I didn't have to say anything they could read the book and I could just crawl into bed and get as much sleep as possible. Since this book is only good for 3 months, I purchased another to send to daycare for the workers to fill it out during the day. When I pick him up, I can see how much he ... Review 16: rating 5.0. great for twins too! loved it! super helpful with twins as well! Review 17: rating 5.0. Great for keeping on top of things Love these books. I have twins and I'd be lost without these! Review 18: rating 5.0. I love this! First, I thought it was kinda pricey. I thought I could use pretty much any notebook. However, it was a real nice investment- it's totally worth it. I've used one for the first 3 months of my son's life, and now i'm on my second notebook. I am planning on using it one more time until he turns 9 months. This will keep your day with the baby a lot more manageable. Trust me, you won't remember what time you last fed your newborn or how many diaper changes you did yesterday.... Review 19: rating 5.0. Daily staple in my household Used this everyday since I brought my now 21 month old son home. Love this book. Review 20: rating 3.0. The format of the 24 hour cycle on each page ... The format of the 24 hour cycle on each page doesn't line up to a calendar date, which is confusing. Each page cuts across two calendar days, but you only have space on the top of each page to write a single calendar date. I wish they had lined the pages starting at 12 am, rather than 6 am.
persona: This customer prioritizes functionality, durability, and practicality in everyday products, seeking items that reliably perform their intended purpose, especially in high-use or high-value scenarios like protecting strollers or enabling child participation at the table, while being sensitive to usability flaws and value for money.
reference_output: rating 1.0. One Star Don't bother getting this- just use pen and paper!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. One Star Does NOT fit a platform bed. Review 2: rating 5.0. works with IKEA platform bed frame but not with pillowtop mattress Didn't work with my pillow top mattress because the mattress was too thick and the railing not high enough. My babies sleep on a queen mattress on a IKEA platform bed and YES it works with a platform bed (the design for this is pretty smart) but because using it with a platform bed, the railing is set even lower and then with the height of the pillow top, it stuck out just 1 inch from the mattress and was no use. Othe... Review 3: rating 5.0. He finally slept in his own bed :) Delivery was fast and item arrived in excellent condition. It is sturdy enough to withstand my son's back and forth wiggling...for now :) He has a platform crib/bed which is 5 inches deep and it was a bit tricky to make the rail work. We added two pieces of 1 inch plywood on top of the bed base (under the mattress) to make the depth less than 5 inches and it worked like a charm. My son likes this rail better than the original crib front panel becaus... Review 4: rating 5.0. Works with platform bed. Yes! Works with platform bed. We have the IKEA Hemnes trundle bed and this works perfectly. The bed has slats, but when it's pushed in, the slats have no spaces between them, so it's like a platform. We have a semi- floppy mattress- natural latex, and no box spring. Still works. It finds down which makes it a little easier to make the bed. It's a daybed, so it's still not super-easy, but that's has nothin grind worth the rail. My 2-y.o. Grabs it and rocks bac... Review 5: rating 5.0. Your search stops here. Your search for the perfect bed rail stops here. It is very solid and is easy to install. We use it on a twin size bed for a 2-year old. It covers the side of the bed that she sleeps on, and leaves a large gap at the foot of the bed, but that hasn't been an issue as we want her to be able to get in and out.

The top of the box spring sits below the bed frame on our bed and this still collapses almost flush with the bed because the hinges are raised... Review 6: rating 4.0. Good choice for the price. My 22 month old decided that he preferred a toddler bed to a crib; however, my convertible crib/toddler rail attachment had been discontinued and I didn't feel inclined to spend $150 on something that might not be compatible. This was a perfect choice. It was fairly simple to install (minimal aggravated noises or words from the husband), but it did require 2 sets of hands. It also requires both hands to push in the sides to fold the rail down. My little... Review 7: rating 4.0. Easy to Install and Easy to Use I love the look and design of this rail. However, BE CAREFUL when you first install it. I lengthened the strap to get it under the bed and it was bunched up under the mattress. As I tried to tighten it, the strap was stuck between the mattress and slats of the bed so the little plastic pieces that guide the strap near the part where you pull broke in half. I was still able to tighten the strap and it functions perfectly, just wish those little pieces h... Review 8: rating 5.0. Works like a charm!!! It's the perfect fit for my son's crib! Review 9: rating 5.0. I love that it can swing down during the day and ... This fits perfectly on my daughter's Land of Nod Hampshire toddler bed. I love that it can swing down during the day and stay firmly up while she is sleeping. The only downside is that the rail makes it difficult to make the bed because every time I lift the mattress up to put on the fitted sheet, the arms that hold the rail down come up and I have to re-position it. Still well worth the nuisance because the rail is awesome! Review 10: rating 5.0. Can use in bed with or without box spring, easy to put together, sleek Oh my gosh, this rail is awesome. We got another highly rated one first but it didn't fit in a platform bed without a box spring. It was also much harder to put together. This one is easy to put together and install, has a sleek look, functions exactly as we had hoped. I didn't initially buy it because I wanted a white rail, but this grey is really subtle and goes nicely with our toddler's white wood twin bed. Review 11: rating 5.0. Great bedrail for kids This is a fantastic bedrail. It works great with our son's 11" thick mattress and still has clearance under it when it folds down. This allows it to lay flat against the bed. Review 12: rating 4.0. Works for a slat bed as the regalo one did ... Works for a slat bed as the regalo one did not. Price was a little hefty, but it works and can withstand my son pulling on it. Worth it! I just wish it was a little longer for the price. Review 13: rating 3.0. Not for use on cribs I purchased this bed rail to use on my son's convertible crib as we transitioned to a toddler bed. I didn't realize that the rail's instructions would indicate it was not for use on cribs -- which I guess mine is still considered, even though it is convertible. When we installed it as designed, and hooked it to the opposite side of the mattress, the entire mattress (rail and all) easily slipped off the frame and onto the floor with my toddler's absent-minded tugg... Review 14: rating 2.0. Be sure to get all the Parts! They left out one of the two arms out. Once we got that piece it worked fine. Review 15: rating 5.0. perfect for crib and now we are using it for ... perfect for crib and now we are using it for twin bed. little shorter than the avergae size of big bed rail, but still good enough to protect kid from falling off of bed. sturdy, easy to assemble. Review 16: rating 4.0. Works Great so far Works Great so far... Wish it had a better way to hold a lighter crib mattress. Easy setup and works well when we took the front of the crib off. Review 17: rating 5.0. Great size. Lightweight Great size. Lightweight. Works with platform bed. Folds completely down & flat. Easy to operate & simple to assemble. Review 18: rating 3.0. Too short for CalKing bed Too short for our California king bed. Keeping it for her toddler bed in the future. Our matteress is pretty high, requiring at least 20inch deep pockets for fitted sheets. Review 19: rating 5.0. Fit IKEA bed Great! Fit with IKEA bed which was difficult to find. Review 20: rating 4.0. No sharp parts I like that this doesn't have any sharp parts that could cut or snag a little boy and his clothes or bedding. It is easy to install and use. It isn't super long, so we've had several instances of falling out of bed, but he is also a pretty active sleeper.
persona: This customer prioritizes ease of installation, functional design, and visual appeal in their purchases, favoring products that deliver reliable performance and aesthetic improvement over time.
reference_output:
rating 5.0. Easy, functional, works. Great, easy to install, looks nicer than comparable products. Has worked well for us since putting our daughter in a daybed. She hasn't fallen out and likes
Sleeping in her bed.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. Substandard. You can feel the difference! The diper broke and gel stuck to my kids butt Sub standard product! I buy the same diapers fro. Costco for some reason the package i received from amazon feel very thin. On the first time my son wore it the gel stuck to his butt which has never happened when i buy the same prodcut from elsewhere Review 2: rating 5.0. Works great for my son he drinks a lot of water these diapers absorb alot and does not cause rash.well for him at least! Excellent quality, quantity is greater, and beats the price at wally-world.I would recommend this to all looking to save without compromising on quality. Review 3: rating 1.0. every time we get out i have to change my ... every time we get out i have to change my daughter clothes because they are leak due to setting on car seat, she is 1yr. i cant keep her dry more than an hour if we got out !! unbelievable!! Review 4: rating 1.0. One Star These diapers are horrible. They leak constantly. Not the same as the diapers I buy in department stores Review 5: rating 1.0. I loved huggies, but after the change I loved huggies, but after the change....If I could leave zero stars, I would! I have these ordered monthly(subscription). Huggies changed the design and now my baby is getting rashes and she even tells me herself that "diaper hurting me" keep in mind my baby only wears these overnight,(advertised as 12hr protection) I have to change her twice over night. She sleeps 7-8 hrs!!! Review 6: rating 1.0. Disgraceful! 0 stars!! POOR QUALITY! I just got a new box of size 5 diapers with a now new design. (Pictured) In the past 8 months They were the colorful design. I haven't had any problem from size 1 til 5 up until this box. The quality is beyond disgraceful. Super thin. I feel like it's going to fall apart. Does not hold my son's urine well. Lots of leaks. Seems like the quality went out the door!!! Review 7: rating 1.0. Is only good for absorbing Urine Is only good for absorbing Urine. No match for Infant Poop on the other hand. If you have these diapers, make sure you have as much extra clothes to change your infant. Its going to get messy. Review 8: rating 1.0. Not so good. Not the same diaper as size 5 at all. Loose and does not fit well. Review 9: rating 2.0. Lower quality than most Huggies I purchased these in size 6 and they are much thinner than all of the other Huggies I've purchased. Review 10: rating 1.0. I really did not like these brand of huggies diapers I really did not like these brand of huggies diapers. I normally buy from Costco, but decided to give amazon a try, boy I wished I had just bought them from Costco. This set of diapers that I got from huggies, were very cheaply made and did not absorb as well as the Huggies that I normally buy from Costco, I will definitely stick to Costco brand huggies from now on. Would not buy again. Review 11: rating 1.0. One Star Disappointed in these, big time! The diapers were so SMALL. A great deal for lousy product. Review 12: rating 1.0. and to make matters worse the price went up from $24 to $37 Last box I received 2 days ago is completely different, it has a weird chemical smell, and it has that yellow line to indicate wetness, which in my opinion is pointless to have for size 6 diapers. It leaked both nights I used them and my daughter wakes up soaking wet and that never happened with the old Huggies. and to make matters worse the price went up from $24 to $37, I had to cancel our monthly subscription because I am... Review 13: rating 5.0. best diapers by far pampers are squishy when they are wet. I hate how they feel. they also have really annoying tape.
huggies feel like cloth instead of plastic...the tape is velcrow instead of sticky...they fit so nice! superior by far. (and they hold more too.) Review 14: rating 1.0. I normally don't review products but... I have 4 small children and I buy a massive amount of diapers. I am also very frugal as I am a stay at home mom. Huggies ARE much more pricey but have always been worth it for me to not have to deal with leaks and lots of laundry. Huggies seemed to be the only diaper I could find that could ensure that my children make it through the night without a diaper change in the night or a sheet change in the morning. I noticed recently that my girls' p... Review 15: rating 1.0. Be careful every one. This is product is totally ... Be careful every one. This is product is totally different and less quality than the one you buy from stores. I have been using this brand more than two years but this was my first time to purchase it online. It so light and does not absorb liquid properly. Review 16: rating 1.0. The worst diapers. They barely stay on when my toddler ... The worst diapers. They barely stay on when my toddler moves around. They leak constantly and soak through his clothes even when it’s not full! If I hold him on my hip, my shirt gets wet. I’ve been using pampers snugglers for years but because this pack has more per box, I switched over. Huggies has always been a well known brand so I was expecting more and switching back. Review 17: rating 1.0. Horrible diapers. Read the other reviews Horrible diapers. Read the other reviews. Theses diapers are so thin and do not hold much urine at all. I will never order these again. I love the little movers by Huggies and will order those next time. Review 18: rating 2.0. Cheaply made—too thin and tabs rip off. These diapers are flimsy and thin. The tabs have ripped off multiple times while trying to tape them on. We were expecting the same quality that we get when we buy Huggies from Costco but this was not what we got. Will not purchase again. Review 19: rating 2.0. Day use only, does not last through the night Very thin diapers and they do not last through the night. They're fine for day use, but we won't buy them again. Review 20: rating 5.0. Size 6 works great for my 50 lb dog I use this size 6 baby diaper for my 50 lb dog with incontinence. I just cut a triangle for the tail, then fold tape over the bottom edge of the cut (so that the urine doesn't leak out of the cut region). This has worked better for me than ANY actual doggy diaper. It has alo worked better in staying on and not slipping off my dog than the huggies little movers diaperes.
persona: This customer prioritizes performance and reliability in diapers, especially leak protection and comfort, and is willing to pay for products that deliver consistent, trustworthy results, though they may be sensitive to functional flaws that compromise safety and dryness.
reference_output: rating 2.0. Two Stars something is wrong with these diapers. the insides clump up when there is even a small pee

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Works Great for Me I purchased two cheaper baby gates at the store only to return them. One was just too cheap to use. The other I returned because there was a gap at the top and I thought that was a product defect.

The Regalo gate is easy to adjust and easy to operate. My son grabs it and push/pulls the bars like he's in prison so, I can say it's "sturdy" too.

Oh and Regalo was the only one to mention that "the gap/space at the top of the gate is no... Review 2: rating 3.0. Great in theory This was the first gate I bought for my son, and while it does contain him to an area, the gate is so narrow that I can hardly walk through it. I am not a heavy person, Im 5'7" and about 150 lbs (former athlete, decent amount of muscle weight there) and I have to turn sideways a little to fit my hips through it. Also, when you open the gate, there is still a bar going across the ground, so it is a real pain in the ass for sweeping. I bought a different one made by... Review 3: rating 5.0. Baby Gate I love this baby gate. I bought it to block off the laundry room because my cat's litter box is in it and my baby is on the go. I also liked the fact that it is made in America. You can put it which ever way you want the door to open. My baby has already tried to figure out the latch without success (only because her hands are not coordinated or strong enough) You don't have to screw anything into the wall to put it in a doorway which is important if you are renting like me... Review 4: rating 3.0. Alittle different than I expected We ordered this item from Amazon directly. We ordered 2 of the same gates at the same exact time but received them 4 days separatly. I didnt know that if used at the top of stairs you had to screw into the wall. Im not sure if it said that or not in the description online. I had purely wanted a pressure mounted one to avoid putting holes into our freshly painted walls and if I knew this I would have bought the all wood gate from Target that anchors a... Review 5: rating 2.0. typical China made product Just received product, it appears that it will work for the intended purpose.

However, if you care about the look of your home do not buy this. The paint on the welds of this gate is already peeling right out of the box. Also the gate is painted bright white while the extension section is painted off white, so that the gate doesn't match with itself!

Further the extension does not sit squarely with the gate and rides up at an angle. Th... Review 6: rating 4.0. Good but small gate opening Good gate for our 9-month old daughter. We have it in the door opening for the basement stairs. I have only 2 gripes about it. The first is that the opening of the gate is very narrow and the second is the rail that you have to step over to get through the gate. Otherwise it's sturdy and easy enough to open. Review 7: rating 2.0. my toddler can open it with ease I used to like this gate, easy to open and services the purpose. Now my son is 25 months and he just figured it out how to open it with ease. I think it's more for babies instead of toddlers. Review 8: rating 2.0. Buy only if your doorway/pathway is within standard length Do not buy this product id you have to use the extension or if the screws supporting the gate project out too much. I tried putting in for a 38" pathway and it does not install properly. Review 9: rating 2.0. Consider before ordering 1. The gate door is simply not as easy to open & close as described. With some practice you can unlock it one-handed, but the latch nearly always catches on the gate requiring two hands to pull the edges away from each other to get clearance. We tried tightening the spindle rods at either end to counteract this problem, but that just makes the gate less stable.
2. The gate opening is extremely narrow. I bump my hip on it every time I walk through, even t... Review 10: rating 2.0. Had to return as the one in the picture was not as advertised Chose this one explicitly because it has a tension mount but when it arrived it needed to be screwed into the wall, which we can't do as the wall in question has marble edging, which I'm definitely not drilling into.
Disappointing... Review 11: rating 5.0. Great for even dogs! I bought two of these gates for our indoor dogs. The included expandable gate makes it a great buy when moving from room to room.

The construction of the gate it well. The paint is solid and doesn't rub off even when scratched. The rubber ends hold tight and the locking mechanism is solid. Sometimes our dogs would even jump on them and lean on it from excitement, and the gate still held up.

If you do place this on wood flooring, I recommend ... Review 12: rating 5.0. LOVE this gate! I had bought this gate for the top of our stairs going down to our den. I have had it for about 4 months and it has stayed put even from my one year old shaking it and from my 6 year old who is rough with it. By far the best gate we have owned...and you can not beat the price. Review 13: rating 2.0. 18 month old son can knock it down I ordered 2 of these to keep my 18 month old out of the kitchen well after 2 weeks he realized he could run into them and knock them down. They are nice but not as sturdy as I would have hoped. They do not stay very well to the walls even with the cup pieces they send. Review 14: rating 1.0. Does not work for 40" Opening We have an opening that is exactly 40" at the top of our staircase. After installing this gate we found that we could simply push on it and it would fold inward, coming completely detached from the wall. We returned it for a more sturdy model made to fit larger spaces. Review 15: rating 5.0. We recently purchased this gate and love it. Our opening was about 39 inches and ... We recently purchased this gate and love it. Our opening was about 39 inches and it fits perfectly. It's quick to assemble and easy to use. We didn't want to drill holes in our walls to install a gate so this was perfect for us. Review 16: rating 2.0. Not as great as my other gate I bought this gate for a friend with children. The construction is not as solid feeling as another gate I own. The latch is kind of a pain to open when you have your hands full. One positive is that it stays open instead of swinging closed. The opening is not very wide either. Review 17: rating 4.0. Good gate, some design problems Overall this is a really good item and serves the purpose well. I use it to prevent my dogs from running into rooms that they are not allowed to go into. The gate is made of metal and seems fairly sturdy. I have been using it for several months and it holds up well overall. The only issue I have seen so far is that it is hard to get it set up and level. If you are not level when you mount this the gate does not close well and as a result the bottom met... Review 18: rating 5.0. Wow! I'm impressed! One of the hardest things to do is to judge the quality and functionality of a product online. We needed a gate for a new puppy and tried to do more than simply close our eyes and throw a dart. The reviews for this product were pretty good, and the price was excellent, and when it indicated that there was even a way to walk through it without taking the whole thing apart, that sealed the deal.

Well, we couldn't be happier with our purchase. The gate is ... Review 19: rating 2.0. not to sturdy when you tighten the ends it pushes the gate closed and you cannot open it.would am going to try diferent style Review 20: rating 2.0. persona: This customer prioritizes functional design and practicality in products that enhance daily caregiving routines, values items that solve specific usability challenges such as accessibility and space efficiency, and is willing to purchase multiple units if a product delivers consistent, reliable performance across different usage scenarios.
reference_output: rating 1.0. I was so happy to get a walk through gate after having to ... What a letdown. I was so happy to get a walk through gate after having to step over ours for a year and this one was a real disappointment. The closure mechanism is far too easy to work. My 5 year had it figured out almost immediately and it didn't take my just turned 3 year much longer. If we don't use the "lock" function and close it the easy way even my baby can open it, she figured it out by 14 months. Thankfully, if we lock it down she is still too short to work the mechanism.  We had a different brand a few years ago and it was much better, so sad a piece got lost while moving and I had to replace it with this one.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 2.0. Too soft for a baby. This was softer/more smushy than I would have expected for a baby mattress. I would not have felt safe putting a newborn or infant on this because it is not firm at all. I didn't even like it for our toddler. Sent it back. Review 2: rating 5.0. Pack n Play mattress The mattress fit perfectly and my infant sleeps well on it. I received it promptly and would recommend it to others. Review 3: rating 5.0. well worth the money imo Got it a week ago but i was waiting on the crib, which came in today. Im using it in a davinci alpha with geranimals play yard sheets. It all fits perfectly and my 6 week old seems to really like it. Much more than the inch thick foam pad in his bassinet. Its much heavier than i had expected, but i think that speaks to the quality. Review 4: rating 5.0. Grandaughter loves it This matress really worked out well. My grandaughter sleeps for longer periods now during the night. needless to say her parents also appreciate the longer sleep that they can now enjoy. Review 5: rating 5.0. Worth every penny This mattress fit perfectly! I had been given an older portacrib with a thin mattress pad. This is the kind of crib you would see at a daycare center. This mattress not only fits but gives great support for my baby girl. Its firm, but not too firm to where you can feel the coils. I would recommend this mattress to anyone with a portacrib. Review 6: rating 4.0. Like a regular crib mattress More like a regular crib mattress. Softer than the 3 inch foam version by this same manufacturer but not too soft. Pay the extra $10 for this one if you're looking for a mini crib mattress. Review 7: rating 4.0. Great mattress - here are the sheets that fit it best A lot of people ask about what sheets to buy for this. I bought these sheets on Amazon for this mattress and they fit perfectly:
American Baby Company 100% Cotton Value Jersey Knit Fitted Portable/Mini Crib Sheet, White, 2 Count
Each sheet measures 24" x 38" x 5" Review 8: rating 5.0. better than what comes with porta-crib I highly recommend this mattress if you buy a porta-crib because the mattress that comes with a porta-crib is thin, flimsy and seems uncomfortable (and too soft if you want to prevent SIDS). I am using the portacrib as a regular crib because I have to squeeze two kids into one room until we sell our house, so I wanted a decent mattress. Babies R Us sells portacrib sheets (Koala baby brand) that fit it, too. Review 9: rating 5.0. Perfect fit for the Dream On Me Aden Convertible Mini Crib! This mattress was perfect for what we ordered it for. It fit the Dream On Me 3 in 1 Aden Convertible Mini Crib perfectly with no gaps on any sides. It's firm yet comfortable enough for a newborn. My son has been sleeping on it for 4 months now and we have had no problems. He sleeps great on it. It's firm, safe, fits the mini crib perfectly, and arrived fast. What more could we want. Great product!
Also I've found that t... Review 10: rating 5.0. Love it! So far I have only just gotten this item, to replace a cheaper mini/portable crib mattress that I had had for less than a year before it started to fall apart. I ordered this item on Dec 6, 2013 pretty late in the evening. I got it today, Dec 9, around 5:45pm. I am VERY pleased with the shipping speed. The mattress feels like it will hold up much better than the mattress it is replacing, and it feels like it will be much more comfortable for my 20 month old son. It fits in t... Review 11: rating 5.0. It's good quality. unfortunately I just bought a wrong size It's good quality.unfortunately I just bought a wrong size.sorry for my own mistake. Review 12: rating 5.0. Two thumbs up I worried when I received this package in the mail because the size of the box made it look like it was a full size mattress! I was so happy when I opened it to see that was not the case! The mattress fits perfectly in our portable crib and is just the right thickness, much better than the flimsy mattress (more like pad) that came with outhe crib. The mattress has some give for comfort but is still firm enough to provide safety. I am very pleased with this mattress. Review 13: rating 4.0. Nice. I was concerned buying the mattress for the Delta Children's Reily Crib, but it fit perfectly. At first mattress looks very flimsy and too thin, but once you put it in it is good enough for at least a year. I'm happy with the purchase and my daughter seems to be very comfortable. Review 14: rating 5.0. Sleep all night My baby never slept so good. I bought this mattress @ 4 months. The mat that came with the crib just wasn't good enough after he started rolling around. Review 15: rating 4.0. Nice Mattress Mattress fits well in my mini crib. The outside stiching is not so good, poorly done. I do not think it will be a problem, it just does not look good. Review 16: rating 5.0. Fit perfect I bought this mattress for my daughters Greco pack and play and it fit perfect. We were going on a long vacation where she was going to be sleeping in her pack and play for over a months and I wanted to make it more comfortable for her, she slept great! Review 17: rating 5.0. so its fine. Was skeptical, for Graco Pack n Play... Slight gap, but my baby sleeps in the middle of his bed, so its fine. Review 18: rating 5.0. Firm and the perfect size for the Da Vinci mini rocking crib! This mattress is just what I needed. Thank you! Review 19: rating 5.0. Perfect for Dream On Me 3 in 1 Mini Crib This fit perfectly in the crib, no spaces left around the edges. Comfortable, I say since the baby sleeps in it throughout the night. Only 3 inches, but enough support. Not soft, but just right. Slippery of course to put the sheet on it, but it worked and hasn't slipped off the matress. Satisfied! Review 20: rating 5.0. Very Pleased with Product We have a Delta Fold Away 3-in-1 Portable Crib from my son that we plan on using as our primary crib for soon-to-arrive daughter. The mattress arrived quickly (thanks to the free Prime shipping option from the Amazon Mom promotion) and fits very snugly in our crib. Mattress seems firm, but not uncomfortably stiff- great proudct for the money.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Great cup! I don't leave many reviews but I always read them! But after reading reviews for sippy cups for hours and buying 10 different types I found the only cup ill ever buy again! I bout the older version of this cup first and fell in love. It had a few flaws, I didn't like the flip top lid, but th is one is great. I've had 4 kids now and never knew that straw cups were so much better developmentally until my last. Finding ngf a good easy to clean straw cup has been hard but thi... Review 2: rating 5.0. Best straw cup for baby yet I have two girls 19 months apart, so I’ve tried many straw cups in the last 2.5 years. Got these in the mail yesterday and already love them more than Munchkin weighted straws. I will update this review if anything changes!

Dr. Brown’s vs Munchkin:

-the weight is rubber, compared to plastic on the Munchkin cup. This makes for a much quieter cup when being shaken or thrown (we all know babies love to throw things lol). It’s also one pi... Review 3: rating 1.0. Leaks Leaked all over and I made sure the straw was in correctly Review 4: rating 2.0. Leaked We loved the old style and were looking forward to the changes on this style, but unfortunately it leaks all over the place! Also the straw doesn’t come out as much as the old style. Review 5: rating 1.0. Too hard to suck I couldn’t even get the liquid to come up the straw and my toddler definitely can’t. Usually like dr brown products but this one defective. Review 6: rating 1.0. Very hard for a baby to get water out Only one of the two cups barely “worked”. One of the cups straw doesn’t work you suck on air even though the bottle has water & yes I had everything put together correctly!!!! The one that does work it is extremely hard for baby to get the water out!! I tried it myself after noticing my baby wasn’t actually drinking anything. It was so hard even for me to get a few drops of water out. Don’t waste your money on this cup. Review 7: rating 5.0. BETTER THAN MUNCHKIN Compared to the munchkin weighted straw cups my daughter had been using since she was 4 months old… these are SO MUCH BETTER!!! I wish I found these sooner! My daughter is a few weeks shy from a year old and she LOVES THESE CUPS! She can drink from it easily, liquid isn’t spewing out the straw, she adores the little picture of the animal on each cup. And a big plus is it holds a lot more liquid it’s also easier for her to hold!!! Definitely easier to clean! I wil... Review 8: rating 5.0. Leak Proof, Easy to Clean, and Easy to Use My daughter figured out how to use this cup in less than 10 seconds and it was her first time using a straw! Leak proof, durable, easy to clean. I love this cup! Review 9: rating 2.0. These leak cold liquids…Not like the old style These cups leak terribly when they have cold liquid in them (milk!). We used Dr. Brown’s weighted straw cup with my older son and liked them so I figured we would try these. They are a slightly different design and in my opinion not as good as the older cups were. They are durable. We might use them as water cups and only serve room temp water in them but unfortunately our quest for the perfect straw cup continues. Review 10: rating 1.0. Can't pull water through the straw! What in the world?! I was wondering why my 12 month old wasn't drinking with these new cups I bought so I tried them out. I couldn't suck water through the straw! It was SO hard to get even a drop of water for me so why would I expect a 12 month old to be able to. I dissembled, reassembled, followed the directions to a T and still nothing. Do not waste your money! Review 11: rating 5.0. My favorite straw cup! So I have been looking for a straw cup for my 12 month old for a while. We have tried a few different ones and I didn’t love them. We have ones that are completely spill proof with a small gasket in the straw but it’s hard to suck liquids out of it, the straws are also hard plastic so I did not feel comfortable with our daughter having the cup alone while she played because I didn’t want her to hurt herself on the hard straw. This cup is pretty spill proof but ... Review 12: rating 2.0. Not Sturdy Liked these cups initially but they do leak and one of our straws got a small hole shortly after purchase making them unusable. We ended up throwing both away Review 13: rating 1.0. Pump out all the liquid Very disappointed, our toddler made the whole house sticky buy leaking the juice while walking holding this cup, because the baby sucks the straw then the liquid pumps out by itself all the time, it drove us crazy besides its very hard to clean the tube we couldn’t do it that we have to trash it eventually and thats all after a month I think, not recommended at all. Review 14: rating 2.0. Can't suck liquid out easily It's very hard to suck anything out of these. We only tried it with water and still had a hard time. I even had my 4yo try it just to see and she said it was very hard to get anything out as well. The other issue is that the water just sits in the straw when just sitting there and will leak that part out when moved. I can't recommend. Review 15: rating 1.0. Cups leak like crazy Cups leak no matter what. Have had this issue before w dr brown cups but wanted to give another try. What a waste of money. Do not buy. Review 16: rating 5.0. Great cup Love this straw cup. Note - in order for it to be leakproof, you must pull the upper straw attachment all the way through the opening until it catches. Review 17: rating 1.0. Doesn’t work Our cup was defective; the straw didn’t work. Disappointing. Review 18: rating 1.0. Leak proof? I wish! I don't understand all the positive reviews claiming these are spill proof. Both of ours leak like a crazy! Even when closed they leak!!!! I had one closed sitting on my coffee table and the juice was leaking out from under the lid. I'm not sure if we got 2 defective ones or what but these are absolute garbage definitely do not recommend. Review 19: rating 5.0. FINALLY Finally a straw cup that doesn't leak everywhere and doesn't require the suction of a Dyson vacuum to drink from! We've tried other weighted straw, no leak cups and my daughter would get so frustrated because she had to work so hard to get anything out. I love that this one has handles and drinks like a normal straw. I'm also happy this cup shows ounces and milliliters so I can see how much she's drinking. My only complaint is the "gendered" color options. Come on, Dr. Brown.... Review 20: rating 1.0. Horrible! These leak! It’s just sitting on the table and the milk is just creeping up the straw and pouring out the top. These are awful! Don’t waste your money!
persona: This customer values practical, functional products that solve specific parenting challenges, prioritizes ease of use and secure fit, and is influenced by trusted recommendations; they favor items that integrate seamlessly into their routine and enhance safety and convenience without complicated setup.
reference_output: rating 5.0. great transition from bottle my daughter was holding her bottle at 4 mos, so when we transitioned, the first thing i liked about this product was that you could remove the "arms". I couldn't say it's completely leak proof- the remaining liquid in the straw will "leak" out if tipped over. but other than that, if you hold upside down, it will not leak any liquid from the cup and it has the protective cap that slides to cover the straw when not in use. it is easy to clean, but like classic dr brown a pain for some people because of the parts. you have to take it apart and clean with that fun mini brush! honestly I don't mind, but it deters people. my daughter preferred this over the other version that you have you hold up to the mouth. she had more control with the amount of liquid she was allowing and prevents gas bubbles. Overall happy with this product!

reviews:

BUT *read for a good tip on how to prevent leaks I have used Dr. Browns for my son since he was born- I love them and will use them again for my next child (well, we switched from Playtex early on). I have a little technique to stop the bottles from leaking- which they tend to do a lot if the milk goes up the center straw. But for some reason these new nipples a leaking ALL THE TIME. :o(

**TECHNIQUE TO STOP BOTTLES FROM LEAKING**
-connect bl... Review 5: rating 1.0. Not recommended The nipple was too soft, after a few sucks it would collapse and have to be released from my son's mouth before sucking again. I think it is a duplicate product I got a LOW QUALITY product, and it did not sustain even for one sterilazation .. i trashed it . I do not recommend this product at all. I think it is a duplicate product Review 14: rating 1.0. Only 5 nipples The nipples are good but I only got 5 nipples. Review 15: rating 2.0. Easy to bite through So thin babies bite holes in them easily Review 16: rating 2.0. Can’t use 1/3 Too bad I can’t use 1/3 of this because THEY’RE CONNECTED TO EACH OTHER! Review 17: rating 5.0. Great quality and value Got the Level 3 for my 10-month-old who I have finally fully weaned from breastfeeding and it was too much for her. The Level 2 is perfect for her! Nipples came in a bag in a sealed Dr. Brown's box and are perfect. Paid $8.99 for six versus $4.99 for two at retail stores. Great quality and great value. Review 18: rating 1.0. The picture shows the old style nipples with the flat bottom but the product delivered is the newer self-venting (wavy-bottom) nipple! I desperately need more of the old style nipples and I am so annoyed after opening a box with the new style nipples!! Ugh!!!! Review 19: rating 1.0. Quality product Great price for 6. Good quality. Fit product perfectly.
persona: This customer prioritizes product integrity and unboxing experience, with little tolerance for defects; they expect items to arrive in perfect condition and are likely to abandon purchases or initiate returns if quality control fails.

So this product is well designed, but I think that a big part of the problem is that the top part that screws on only takes a quarter tur... Review 2: rating 5.0. Favorite cup for around the house I purchased 5 different styles of sippy cups to help transition my son from bottles. Soft spouts were OK but mostly used for chewing on, he liked the soft straw cup but didn't really drink much out of it before starting to chew on it as well. Luckily, the Munchkin 360 was an immediate hit! He figured out how it works quicker than I did and was soon drinking out of it in all positions: one handed, two handed, upside down, you name it. And my son looks... Review 3: rating 1.0. Gets Moldy At first we loved these cups, but after a few months, we noticed mold building up in on the inside ring of the cup. Review 5: rating 3.0. Still on the hunt for a sippy cup I have 11 month old twins that have been harder to find the right cup with. Their older brother took to a straw cup at 8 months and then transitioned to a regular cup at roughly 18 months. No muss, no fuss. The twins are not being as easy on us. I've tried several straw cups but they mostly want to play. I found these cups and thought it was s great idea. The goal is to get them to a cup anyway. They figured out how to drink from it quickly and it's ... Review 6: rating 2.0. These cups work great for drinking These cups work great for drinking. They don't spill. But if you have a child that doesn't throw cups, this is a good one. Review 7: rating 4.0. These cups were recommended by dentists so I purchased it These cups were recommended by dentists so I purchased it. It took awhile for my daughter to get how to use it, but after getting the hang of it, sipping stuff out of it was a piece of cake. The reason why i knocked it down one star is the fact that it's not completely leak proof. If you kid has a tendency to throw stuff, like this cup, the contents will come out. Oh and FYI: make sure when you clean the cups to remove, clean ... Review 8: rating 1.0. Mold that can't be avoided. We loved these cups so much at first that we bought two sets. Review 1: rating 5.0. Five Stars Very safe in the day and time Review 2: rating 5.0. Five Stars Baby and mammy love it. Just follow the directions (included in the package and also posted on Amazon and on the Boba website) and you'll be ok. I would definitely recommend this for use with a newborn. Review 15: rating 5.0. Don't waste your money on any other wrap Don't waste your money on any other wrap, sling, carrier, etc... As a result it really bunched up and rode up my shirt in the back. That was annoying. Also baby kept sliding down and I was worried she'd not be able to breathe as well. It just needs to be a little less stretchy. It seems to be made out of good material though an... Review 20: rating 5.0. I Can Use Both Hands I was hesitant to get it at first since it didn't appear as convenient to put on and take off as some other baby carriers. I love this wrap. The videos online do a great job explaining how to tie the wrap as well as how to position your baby. I would highly recommend th...

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Pretty awesome plates We needed new plates for daily use and I had no idea that eco-friendly tableware was even a thing. But as we transition to more sustainability in our home, I did a search for eco-friendly plates – and I think this is by far the best. I LOVE the colors and what I love the most is these are MICROWAVE SAFE! I also like how easy it is to clean. Pretty awesome plates. I am very impressed. Review 2: rating 5.0. Perfect size! These are perfect for our toddlers! My 2 year old loves these plates! Review 3: rating 5.0. Great product My kids love the colors and I love that they aren’t plastic. Work great! Review 4: rating 5.0. Great product! These plates are easy to clean, very sturdy and durable, and are microwave and dishwasher safe. What makes them even better is that they're made of bamboo and are biodegradable. I would highly recommend these. I love that we get one of each color. The only thing I'd want is a set of four of the same color, or a set of pastel/softer colors. Maybe even some with cute baby-friendly designs on them. Excited to find out what this company comes out with next. Review 5: rating 1.0. Not durable Second day a plate broke off a huge chunk just sitting in the sink waiting to be cleaned. I bought these for his dinners but didnt look at the dimensions. In my case we now use these as snack plate only because my son eats a lot of food and these just aren't big enough. They do great in the dishwasher. Review 7: rating 5.0. Awesome Great product, great mission, great customer service!! Review 8: rating 5.0. Worth every penny Love these!! Worth every penny! Great product! We use these for every meal! Easy to clean dishwasher & microwave safe. Win Win Review 20: rating 2.0. I thought it was due to me putting the plates on the bottom rack of my dishwasher. Then I literally watched a plate crack as a result to the heat from the food I had just placed on it. The same thing happens with the bowls. The plates crack when they heat up...I already had to throw away three of the four ...

reference_output: rating 5.0. Highly recommend I’m so happy I found this brand. I’ve been frustrated because most children’s plates and bowls are plastic or not microwave safe. reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Wonderful formula for our baby Wonderful formula for our baby, and I researched extensively. However, their new Whey Protein version gets my 5 star rating. Review 2: rating 1.0. I also read great reviews, so I thought I would give this ... I read a lot of reviews saying this could constipate your baby. I also read great reviews, so I thought I would give this formula a try on my 7 month old. After just being on this formula for one day, she was not herself. Very fussy and was already having BM issues. She had two BM and they were already getting very thick. This formula was horrible for my baby.

I ordered Hipp and Holle previously from Europ... Review 3: rating 4.0. Best formula in the country. Baby is happy and healthy. After doing a lot of research on baby formula I found that this was the only one without questionable ingredients. It is safe for newborns also and is marketed as a toddler formula to encourage breastfeeding.

My baby is way happier than most brands. He sleeps better and seems satisfied eating less formula. There is much less gas and crying.

The reason I didn't give this 5 stars is because he gets constipate... Review 4: rating 5.0. You have to Measure it out Correctly to avoid the Constipation! This formula is the only one my baby loves.
I had to switch to formula to supplement, now my baby drinks this formula for all meals.
But listen folks - it will cause constipation if you don't measure it out right.

It says on the can - measure One Unpacked Level scoop (8.5 g) for every 2 oz of water.
GET OUT YOUR SCALE.... set it to Grams
My unpacked level scoop was different than what my hu... Review 5: rating 4.0. Soybean oil I don't have knowledge of baby formulas in the market so I can't make a good assessment but I started working so wanted to have an organic baby formula at home for the nanny. I don't see the need of soybean oil in an organic baby formula though. And that's why I gave it 4 stars. Review 6: rating 1.0. Not the best option for infants. This formula is not suitable for infants!! I'm breastfeeding but need to supplement for my 1 month old daughter. I've switched from similac to this thinking it's the best organic one in the US market. It made her irritable and constipated. Only used it for couple of days, will never feed it to my daughter again, it may work for toddlers. Review 7: rating 2.0. Wanted to Love it! But Caused Baby to be constipated! After reading all the reviews and researching. I realized this was the best organic option available in the US. I wanted to order Holle from Europe but my husband talked me out of it! Since I am only supplementing and usually only using formula three or four times a week. So one serving every other day or so, ordering from out of the country seemed ridiculous. My baby seemed to like the taste better than other formula. She was ... Review 8: rating 1.0. Not for newborns Not suitable for a 3 week old, the baby was not able to digest it at all and kept on spitting it out. Review 9: rating 4.0. Changing Price Is Annoying! I really love this formula but I'm really annoyed that the price keeps changing! Review 10: rating 5.0. No fussy baby! My baby was 5 months old when I got pregnant. Though I was exclusively breastfeeding, she began demand feeding every 2-3 hours round the clock. Despite my best efforts to produce more milk, I just couldn't keep up with her needs. So, after lots of research to avoid GMOs, crazy additives and insane food processing practices (using hexane and the like), I found Baby's Only. I started feeding my daughter this formula a week ago. She is now sleeping through the night again... Review 11: rating 5.0. Kid can't get enough of this stuff Great stuff. My kid would give it 6 stars based on how he chugs it. We switched to use this sometimes at 6 months. Never had any issues. I think it smells bad cleaning the bottles but all formula probably does. They should consider selling in bigger containers. Each one lasts a few days I think when he was having four 6 oz bottles a day. Review 12: rating 5.0. Life saver! Just buy it already! we had extreme eczema and after months of trying to find out what the issue was we changed EVERYTHING but the formula( enfamil).. Did some research and found this, tried it and love it! We did have constipation issues, as I saw others did too. I even got a scale and I was measuring correctly, so I just added more water (as I saw someone post) so example: 4 oz water two scoops, I make 5 oz with two scoops and it's been working for him! And his skin has... Review 13: rating 4.0. Good organic formula option Chose this formula due to its great ratings. my LO seems to really like it. As reviewed by few it does make them a bit constipated, but overall seems like it's safe(er) since its an organic formula and rated much better than other organic brands, including E's Best. Be aware this is not with DHA as finding that one has been a bit challenging. Review 14: rating 5.0. It feeds a baby - and fine for infants Ok, it's hard to 'Love' formula - but when you need to augment breastfeeding - or need something to have on hand for long trips - this is great. While it's marked 'Toddler Formula' it meets all the dietary requirements for infants. The company believes that mother's milk is the best for infants - so they don't market to mothers with infants. Very respectable.

I read several reviews about formula - specifically to find out what's reall... Review 15: rating 2.0. It made my poor little boy *very* constipated It made my poor little boy *very* constipated. I'm so happy with this formula! He had been on state provided spit up control formula with corn syrup listed as the first ingredient. I immediately began researching other options knowing this other formula was likely causing or exacerbating his health i... Review 17: rating 5.0. Amazing formula! After research I found that Gerber ( the formula she was on previous to baby's only) had GMO's in their formula. I also learned that DHA&ARA was derived from algae and fungus and then washed with hexane and acid. EW! That is true for all big name baby formula- even some... Review 18: rating 5.0. Best Baby Formula You Can Buy This is by far the best baby formula on the market today. Next time you are at the store, read the ingredients on a conventional brand of formula. It is appalling what they put into them. Even the so-called Organic formulas are not that great!
If you cannot breastfeed or need to supplement, this is the way to go. See more here [...] Review 19: rating 5.0. Based on our research this was one of the better choices on the market Based on our research this was one of the better choices on the market, it has thus far lived up to the expectations. We've been using this as a supplement to breastfeeding and the baby seems to do well with it. Looking forward to trying the new Whey version. Review 20: rating 4.0. This was the closely option I was comfortable with although it still has a good amount of sugar in it. We prefer to use Hipp Organic as the first ingredient is skim milk vs. brown ric...

reference_output: rating 5.0. Great formula! Began using it to supplement when had ... Great formula! Began using it to supplement when had to go back to work. Continuing to pump & BF while with baby, but this formula is great when there isn't enough milk on hand. Baby loves it. No spitting up issues. Not made with dha derived from potentially harmful substances like other formulas. Contains amino acids that convert to dha. Definitely recommend. After much research went with this formula & am very satisfied.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. good suction cup plate for children Liked the product. We use it for feeding our grandson. Review 2: rating 5.0. As described, color (blue) was a match to the picture. My grandson will love it and it will grow with him. Review 4: rating 5.0. Near perfect. Love this plate. Really sticks! Great plates, wish they were a bit cheaper in price Great Plates! easy to clean and i love that its dishwasher safe. Just wish they were a bit cheaper in price. Review 7: rating 1.0. Love these Cute colors, very durable, suction works very well and it’s something my toddler can use st the dinner table and I’m not concerned he will be able to toss it across the room! Great for the toddler with a knack for messes While these plates won't stop your kiddo from dumping food off their fork into their lap, it's a simple, sturdy way to provide multi-course meals to your budding independent eater. Plates are durable, but flexible, clean up well in the dishwasher (without warping) and have strong suction. Honestly, the suction is a little TOO strong at times. It sticks immediately to almost any surface you set it on, even without pressure! Very happy with... Review 15: rating 5.0. Works perfectly! The material is the right thickness for the plate to be flexible yet not flimsy.

Satisfied!

Would not recommend, prefer pampers overnight. Review 6: rating 5.0. and the boy Gods obliviously did not like us! We have tried EVERY diaper on the ... We have tried EVERY diaper on the market; I do mean EVERY diaper, pull-up, and nighttime daytime diaper that has been on the market for the last 20 years (age of our oldest). We do not think that name brand means anything nor do we use the ex... Review 7: rating 1.0. New version is TERRIBLE Leaks everywhere!! We loved the older version, but this new version does not work at all. Switching to Pampers. Review 8: rating 1.0. No good Yup, got the new design and these diapers do in fact leak. Twice this past week, never had a problem with the old kind. Review 9: rating 2.0. new version is not working well I have been using the huggies overnights now for a few years for my children. I have never had a problem until recently. I have noticed (like other reviewers) that in the last few months, my son is now waking up soaked in the morning. I thought it was maybe something we were doing differently but reading all the other reviews I think something is defective with the new material or design. Please bring back the old version as something is not working co... Review 10: rating 1.0. New version is terrible. 1 star is for the new Mickey Mouse version. We used the old Pooh ones for years because they were wonderful overnight diapers. I have 2 year old twins. I bought a lot. The latest shipment was of the "new and improved' Mickey Mouse version. It leaks constantly. It isn't even as good as regular Pampers daytime. I really don't care what you do with the graphics, but bring back the old diaper design. I won't buy anymore until you do. Review 11: rating 1.0. Would not recommend this diaper even during the day Absolutely awful diaper, poorly shaped. My son peed through the diaper on the first night we tried the product. Diaper is not soft to touch. Feels rough as paper. Would not recommend this diaper even during the day. Review 12: rating 2.0. I thought that these diapers were too difficult to put ... I thought that these diapers were too difficult to put on. They wanted to fold into themselves. It wouldn't be a big deal if I weren't a sleep deprived parent of twins, I suppose. I was convinced that these diapers had a conscious and were resisting their inevitable fate. Review 13: rating 1.0. One Star My baby woke up wet every morning. Review 14: rating 1.0. Disappointed. We had been using Pampers Swaddlers and noticing ... Disappointed.

We had been using Pampers Swaddlers and noticing our baby was a little damp in the morning, so I decided we needed to try overnight diapers. I've used them 2 nights and now had 2 major wet spots that soaked through his diaper, pjs, swaddle sack, sheet, and to his mattress pad. Highly disappointed and seriously annoyed to have wasted my money. Review 15: rating 1.0. Mickey is no good. Do not care for new mickey design. The Winnie the pooh were much better. Returning these and looking for other overnights. Few nights of using and baby getting rub burn. Review 16: rating 2.0. Do not absorb well I was hoping that I wouldn't have to wake up for a diaper change in the middle of the night, unfortunately these left my little one feeling wet. Whenever I remove little movers they still feel dry in the inside however these feel soaked. You can feel the wetness they do not absorb well. Review 17: rating 1.0. One Star Terrible product. The regular huggies hold better than this one Review 18: rating 2.0. Bought them because we were hoping the diapers would help ... Bought them because we were hoping the diapers would help our daughter sleep longer. They had the opposite effect as they leaked both nights we tried them. Switched back to the old diapers as at least they didn't leak. Review 19: rating 2.0. Decent but not a repeat purchase Decent diaper but the Kirkland diapers from Costco are more absorbant and work better for us even though it isn't an overnight diaper. I would not purchase this item again. Review 20: rating 5.0. All Hail Huggies OverNites These diapers are worth. every. penny.
Here's the thing. When my first daughter was in diapers, we bought a generic brand from a big box store. We liked one generic brand more than the other generic brands... but ultimately I thought "a diaper is a diaper." Find the one that fits your kid's butt best, and you're golden.
Enter baby number two, who poops and pees with such force and volume we've been hard-pressed to have any kind of diaper ...

persona: This customer prioritizes safety, practicality, and long-term usability in products, especially those for infants and young children, and values solutions that are effective, durable, and easy for parents to manage, even if installation or initial setup is challenging.
reference_output:
rating 4.0. Gentle and effective, great for breastfed babies Starting using this when my twin girls had their first cold at 5 months--as any nursing mom knows, nasal congestion can make it hard for baby to breastfeed effectively. The Occobaby has been a gentle and effective tool. I've found it works best after doing a quick squirt of saline in each nostril and gently massaging the sinus area--or after baby has been in the jumper a few minutes to loosen things up.

The only negative I've found (and why this is 4 stars instead of 5), is that reservoir (snot collector) leaks if you have to angle or move the device around too much during suction, which, with a squirmy baby, you often have to do. I believe with more practice and/or a more mature or calm baby, this may be alleviated.
