Review 1: rating 5.0. Great Fun!!! What a great way to spend an evening with family and friends! It's fun to see how others will respond, sometimes dead-on, other times way off the mark! It's such a simple game anyone can play it. We campaign for our answer to be picked, which does take away from the anonymity, but it's so much fun! Review 2: rating 4.0. No losers game This game is unusual since there are no WRONG answers! There are better and worse answers, but it is all a matter of opinion. If you have shy persons who hesitate to play a game because they are afraid they can't remember the right answer (like Trivial Pursuit), this is a game they will enjoy. Review 3: rating 4.0. Great 'Gettin-to-know-you' Game! We love this game. It is a great game to play when you want to get to know people better. And its very good at getting the conversation moving. We love it. Review 4: rating 4.0. No right or wrong, but fun anyway When my family picked out this game to play on Thanksgiving I thought we would either have a great time or end up bickering over why one persons answer was more right than the other person (leading to a holiday meltdown).....Luckily we did not experience the later. The game is pretty simple, it reminds me of balderdash but there is not right answer. Instead the card will give you an adjective and you have to pick from the 5 or 6 cards you are holding... Review 5: rating 5.0. BUY THIS GAME best game ever!!! The best part is that anyone can play. you can even drank a lot and still play this game, its not complicated at all. Play it at your party and you will have the funnest party around. between this game and madgab i've never had so much fun with a card game. it is as cheap as i could find it here on amazon. so buy it now don't hesitate. Review 6: rating 5.0. Best game ever This is the best game I have ever played. I just bought it for the Church Christmas Gift Drive for a teenager.

I have played Apples to Apples with many different groups of people -- old and young -- and have had a great time, every time. Sometimes I follow the rules, and sometimes we just flip the cards and make up our own game. Only problem is I've been through all the cards so many times, there are not too many surprises. It will always be fun though, ... Review 7: rating 5.0. Fun party game Apples to Apples makes a great party game. It is simple to explain/learn the rules, and each "round" is quick and doesn't require much concentration/thinking. Plus, guests can sit out a round or two while they use the bathroom, fix up a new plate of food, take a call on their cells, etc., without everyone needing to delay or them getting too far behind. It tends to lead to some good-natured teasing and trash-talking, and isn't so involved you can't have side convers... Review 8: rating 5.0. If you haven't played this game, you're just not playing games This is one of the absolute best party games ever. I bought A2A at a local game shop on a whim years ago. My Amazon purchase was the big set for a friend and his family. Be prepared for hours of fun.

Here is the general idea:

First, get a group of people wanting some social fun
Second, pass our the Noun cards (they aren't called that, but that's what they are)
Third, start with someone c... Review 9: rating 2.0. Cheaper at Target As my 20 yr. old niece said, "it's more fun with 15 people that have been drinking. You need to have people with a sense of humor or irony. Also, it was much cheaper at Target $15. Review 10: rating 2.0. Overrated Based on all these reviews we were expecting more. Each player gets 7 noun cards and players take turns being the judge. The judge pulls an adjective card, reads it aloud and the other players place their best noun card face down. The judge mixes them up, reads them and picks the winner. First player with X adjective cards wins. You'll get a few laughs (Sultry: Rosie ODonnell or Scintillating: Freckles or Eternal: Rush Limbauch) but we all thought your creativity is too lim... Review 11: rating 1.0. Stupid Waste of Time & Money This has to be THE most inane "game" on the planet. Completely disappointing, since many people have raved about this game. Why it's called Apples to Apples is beyond me, since there are no right answers. The answer is whatever the judge wants it to be. It is a totally frustrating game for anyone with working brain cells. It should be called Word Disassociations.

If you're 12, this game might be for you. I'll stick with games that don't insul... Review 12: rating 5.0. Great Party Game My friends and I do game nights. This game is so much fun, especially if you have a new person that doesn't know everyone. It's a great ice breaker and zero stress. Also since the game is basically cards it's unofficially portable. We grab a stack and take them with us for waiting somewhere. Because there are so many cards we don't run into the same ones over and over. Bought it because it's a must have for every house. Perfect emergency game, no prep and easy t... Review 13: rating 5.0. games of fun i enjoyed playing this game. It is best with a group instead of just two people. i would suggest if your buying a game for a group of people go for this one. Review 14: rating 5.0. Love this game! This is a terrific game! Every time you have a group of people together, you should play this game. It also makes a great gift. When this thing went on sale for $13.99, I bought 2 of them for gifts because we like ours so much.

You can read all of the tons of reviews. I just wanted to add to the score. This is our family's favorite game. Review 15: rating 5.0. So much fun for the entire family My nephew taught me this game. He in his 40s, I in my 50s. We all had such a great time playing this game. It's full of laughs. You don't need to be smart to play this game. You laugh the entire game. That's what makes it so much fun. I turned around and purchased this game from Amazon and invited my sister, brother in law, and grandparents over to show them the game. They all had a great time. This game is fun for ages 10-100 no intelligence needed. Review 16: rating 5.0. Makes a great drinking game in college! I'm 23, a recent college grad, and I have to say I have spent many nights playing apples to apples making it into a drinking game and having so much fun I laugh my butt off. Love this game. Its great. I wish you could play with less than 4 people. But the more the merrier!

persona: This customer prioritizes effectiveness and long-term usability over convenience or aesthetics, willing to invest time in learning proper usage for optimal results, and values products that reduce recurring costs and maintenance.
reference_output:
rating 5.0. Better than Nose Frieda if used correctly! We had been using the Nose Frieda but, although that works well- it's super gross and I was sick of buying filters. If you use a saline spray first and then as other users have said, hold the nose piece up and move around until suction happens and try plugging other nostril- it works amazingly well! It might not seem like it is sucking stuff right away- but trust me- it is!

Once you've cleared out both nostrils, turn the faucet on and suck some water through and it will clear out the boogers into the reservoir and you will see how much gross stuff you've actually gotten out. I think the key is using saline spray. This is significantly more effective than the nose frieda and is faster and way less gross. Just remember to suck some water through- (the reservoir has to still be screwed on to suck up water) to flush it out and to see how much boogers you've gotten. Then unscrew reservoir and rinse out and set out to dry. Our baby girl is much happier with how efficient this is and my husband and i don't have to fight over whose turn it is to suck boogers with the nose frieda.

The infant flow is the slowest flow and even with that nipple we have to really pace our son during bottle feedings as the breastmilk flows out even with the bottle tipped down slightly. So we try to give him constant breaks and to burp him multiple times durin... Review 2: rating 5.0. Love it! I have tried many different bottles over the years. I really dislike these bottles I really dislike these bottles.

1. The shape is so fat and squat that it doesn't seem ergonomic to hold. Further, the bottles have curves and lips so milk gets caught there. In order to get all the milk, you have to stop, swirl multiple times.

He does need to burp afterwards, but he needs to after he nurses as well. We have not n... Review 8: rating 5.0. I love the whole Avent system I love the whole Avent system. I've had problems nursing all three of my children and ended up pumping within the first couple of weeks. I currently have a two week old and have been able to nurse and bottle feed using these bottles easily with minimal fuss. We usually nurse in the mornings and pump the rest of the time due to trying to keep up with my other kids after school and I'm personally just not comfortable nursing in public; and she can be a bit... Review 9: rating 3.0. I like how they have a wide breast shaped nipple however I use these bottles sparingly for travel (because they are convenient with minimal parts). I like how they have a wide breast shaped nipple however, the "dual anti colic valves" don't seem to work like they say. They claim "the valves release air back into the bottle rather than baby's tummy", but I notice that they just accumulate a lot of bubbles in the stem of the nipple and I have to pull the bottle out of b... Review 10: rating 1.0. Nipples not for newborn I am giving this one star because of the nipples. The nipples were too fast for my newborn. He choked on the milk. Review 11: rating 4.0. We've found that these bottles have worked great for our newborn We've found that these bottles have worked great for our newborn. He still spits up--don't believe any bottle that says it completely prevents spit-ups, all babies spit-up somewhat--but they're usually not major and he probably burps more easily thanks to the nipple design. The only complaint is that the shape of the bottle can make it hard to determine how much formula is left once he's done feeding. Review 12: rating 5.0. Review 13: rating 5.0. Recommended & Approved I purchased the Avent bottles because they were recommended as the best option in the Baby Bargains book. - cons: milk comes out too fast, bottle leaks, too expensive, poor design no, i do not recommend this product. persona: This customer prioritizes practicality, versatility, and ease of use in baby products, favoring items that are functional for on-the-go lifestyles, easy to clean, and adaptable to changing needs—especially those that support breastfeeding and flexible parenting routines.
reference_output: rating 5.0. ... believer in trying different bottles to find what is best for your baby I am a firm believer in trying different bottles to find what is best for your baby. My son did like the Madela bottles when first born, but he quickly outgrew them. The nipple would collapse because it was too slow of a flow. He is primarily breastfed, and when giving him stored breastmilk and occasional formula, he loves these bottles! He does however prefer the first stage nipple even at four and a half months. Going to try for stage two soon. Overall, huge fan and have not dealt with any gas issues.

persona: This customer prioritizes multi-functional, durable, and easy-to-maintain products that offer long-term value and versatility across different use cases, and is willing to accept minor trade-offs like size for superior performance and reliability.
reference_output: rating 5.0. 100% worth the money! We have used this so many times. We don’t even use a regular stroller anymore. I’ve used it with the car seat attachment and without. I’ve used it as a bassinet on the go for my newborn, and have even used it outside during “mosquito hours” and the netting worked perfect. It is a little bulky and would be difficult to fit in a small car. But it fits find in the back of my 2010 Town and Country without having to put the seats down and also fits in the back of my husband’s 2005 Ford Escape. My 3 year old, who is tall for her age, also fits just fine in the wagon while my newborn uses the car seat attachment. I’ve had no issues with navigating it and it glides pretty smoothly. I plan on buying the cooler bag and the rain cover for when my oldest has cheer practice/games.

rating 5.0. My 5 month old son had to sleep at his grandparents in their pack n play unexpectedly one night and it was horrible - he thrashe This is a must have for any parent/grandparent/child care provider that will utilize a pack n play.
My 5 month old son had to sleep at his grandparents in their pack n play unexpectedly one night and it was horrible - he thrashed and rolled all night and was a crab the next day. Could not be more satisfied.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Really great buy. [Mom of a 3mo boy born in May, 15lbs.] I was scared at first because it feels like super soft extra thick T-shirt material. Very stretchy. But it works perfectly. Gotta get the hang of putting it on in public because it’s so long it ends up touching the ground while I’m putting it on. (I could put it on before I leave the house but normally I have it “just in case”. It’s so hot outside I try not to wear him while we’re out in that heat. And I don’t want to just wear it in this heat either, lol. Summer is brutal in NC.

A hundred times more comfortable than the older style carrier we had. I used to have shoulder and back pain when he was just 9lbs after an hour with the old carrier. So I had to make a change.

Only con is the older carrier has a head support that allows me to nurse without being obvious, you have to look down into the carrier to even attempt to see anything. Which was great because I could still use my arms and grocery shop while nursing. With this one, since it’s summer I can’t put his head under the thick fabric to nurse. So I have to stop and jimmy rig things instead of just latching him on and going. This is going to be harder in the winter when he’s older since he already prefers sitting up at 3mo. He loves me and all, but he likes being able to wiggle more.

I would totally suggest this to any new Mom because for the price you get something amazing. This is 10% of the price of some carriers out there. And it’s really good quality.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Does what it's supposed to - with no added frills! Like all OXO products, this one is designed to be functional, and it does that great!  We have one on each floor of our house for our toddler to be able to reach all the things he needs to, and even the things he shouldn't :)  It's sturdy (my husband has used it multiple times) nonslip, easy to pick up and move with the integrated handles, and blends in with our decor.

Review 2: rating 5.0. Helping my big girl be independent My almost 2 year old is ready to do everything herself. And that's welcome because she's beginning to be a little heavy for me to hold on my hip as we brush our teeth.  This makes her want to brush. She can see the mirror but she can't quite reach the taps to wash her hands. Still it helps, it's wide enough for her to stand on and light enough for her to carry it around to get into mischief

Review 3: rating 5.0. Works well for multi-uses Works well for my toddlers. It’s light enough that my three year old can move from the toilet to the sink without help. We have tall (over 17”) toilets and this helps make the seat accessible for him. Also doubles well as a stool.

Review 4: rating 5.0. Quality Step I liked the product! Used it to help my get up to my bed when I had surgery with limited mobility. Comes in handy for other things just out of reach!

I can easily move it Having to stretch to reach the second shelves in my kitchen and then breaking 3 beautiful plates I thought it best to get a small stool that I could shuffle around from one corner of my kitchen to the other.  This stool is perfect for that and I have found a place where I can put it when I don't have a need for it.  It adds about six inches to my reach which is enough that I don't often have to get out the small ladder.

Review 6: rating 5.0. Great for toddlers, a good higher stiol5 Love this little stool. It's a little higher in height than the BJorn, which is great. But keep in mind it is for "toddlers" so i try not 2 use it much for myself. I can feel it wouldn't last long if I did. Whereas I've grabbed the Bjorn stool and used it lots without feeling it may not hold up.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Really great gift, pretty easy to use if you follow the instructions - wife loved it! I got this for my wife for her first mother's day and used it with our 4 month old. I saw a lot of reviews complaining about the quality or ease of use. There may have been some quality issues at one point (some of the reviews I saw were a few years old), but there certainly was not with my kit. While the video instructions and things I saw said to mix three mounds together to create the clay, mine came with just one package of clay and it was very moist and easy to use. It does say you can use olive oil if it seems dry, but I did not need to do that. It took me 10 minutes to really roll it out in the frame piece for it and I assume the people complaining didn't take the time to really smooth it out and went through it too quick. TAKE YOUR TIME - it's really not hard to use or difficult at all, but like anything you likely will be giving as a gift or keeping for years and years, take that extra few minutes to smooth it out and don't get frustrated. In my pic, you can see a few lines or wrinkles. I didn't really care and a few of them were a result of me pressing my sons foot and hand in there. I didn't want to attempt to smooth them out after with fears it would mess up the perfect foot and hand print I got. But you can do it if you're careful.

Once I got it smoothed out, it was very easy. I did do what a few people recommended and waited to do this until my son was sleepy/sleeping. It made a big difference. While the clay does NOT set quickly, if you do mess up any of the attempts for the hand or foot print due to your baby moving too much or being fussy, you will have to put them back down, smooth it out again, and start over. So my recommendation is to wait until your baby is sleeping. You do have to press down somewhat firmly but not really that hard. I didn't even disrupt my little guy when doing his foot or hand - and I never felt like it was hurting or would hurt him if he was awake - the consistency of the clay was perfect. And while it is safe for babies, I'd recommend (as the directions do) to have a damp cloth with some mild baby soap just to wash/rinse their hand and foot.

After that, you've done the "hard part". Now you just have to print some pictures, attach (with the double sides tape, what a great idea for them to include this) them to the included mat, and wait 3-4 days for the clay to set to put it together. It said it can take up to 4 days, in some cases 5. Mine was dried within 3 days, but I did wait the full 4 just to make sure. But it seemed to harden up after just a day or so and got much harder as the days went on. I'd absolutely wait the full 4 though because while it may feel hard to the touch, if there is still moisture in there, it could potentially mold or fog up your glass if you frame it too quickly.

A couple of final thoughts:
-The kit is SO nice - literally this company is GREAT and so thoughtful - it came in a nice box/kit with everything you could need. It had multiple mats (two colors), plastic letters to press into the clay (I crossed out my sons name and date for privacy, but the letters are very easy to use, just press firmly in), the clay, a mini roller, double sided tape (for the pictures), a nice frame with glass, very sturdy inset for the clay, and a really nice thank you card (thanking you for buying it). I love this company, they are great!

Please do yourself a favor and if you are even thinking about getting this, just get it. You will love it and it's something that's so special. I saw some people using it for pets too! I will be getting another one shortly for my dog.

Little Hippo - thank you for making such a great kit that helped me produce and commemorate something so special and timeless!

Honestly, they could probably charge 2-3 times the price they are charging for this and I'd still say it was 100% worth it. It brought my wife to tears and it's something we will have forever. I couldn't possibly recommend this more!

persona: This customer prioritizes safety and peace of mind when purchasing baby products, especially those related to sleep, and is willing to invest in high-quality, functionally designed items that support both comfort and developmental mobility while minimizing risk.
reference_output: rating 5.0. This sleep sack provides safe warmth and comfort for my baby This sleep sack provides safe warmth and comfort for my baby while also allowing him the mobility to crawl around and stand up in his crib. He is very strong and mobile and I was comfortable enough to let him use a small blanket at night. But after recently hearing about the heartbreaking story of a mother who lost her 7 month old son even though she believed he was strong and old enough to be safe with a blanket in his crib, I knew I could no longer risk it. Usually I will put the SleepSack on my son if he is in regular pjs and not a zip up sleep-and-play. That way his belly and feet will stay warm all night. Very happy with my purchase, especially at half the price of most baby stores (purchased like-new with open packaging).

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Best! I have twin boys and these are the nipples that i was recommended. They are now 6 month old and these are the only ones i use! Review 2: rating 5.0. No Complaints These work great for us. They are the only nipples we use. When my daughter was about 4 months old, she started sucking harder and faster and the nipple would collapse and become ineffective. When that happens, it's time to switch to the faster flow. We now use the Medium Flow, and those are working great for us. Review 3: rating 5.0. Great for breast-fed babies! I initially ordered these Medela nipples because I already had about 8 or 9 Medela bottles laying around, from purchasing our breast-pump and getting a gift set at my shower. I breastfeed my son, but he gets breastmilk in a bottle while I am at work and he is in daycare. Since this nipple has a wide-base, it seems to be more "true to life," if you know what I mean. I have noticed that he feeds better with me at home and doesn't have any trouble transit... Review 4: rating 1.0. not slow flow at all i got this for m 2 month old. This is not slow flow at all. flow is faster than medium flow nozzle. My baby could not take it. Review 5: rating 5.0. Great for our baby Our 4 month old has been using this brand, and this particular size since birth, and we have had no problems at all with them. They are easy to wash, hold up well to every day use, and I trust the brand. I would recommend these to any new parent. Review 6: rating 2.0. Inconsistent flow If you put water over these nipples you would notice the flow is quite inconsistent. Some would drips while other pours. My baby was really fuzzy while using the medela bottles and it took me a while to notice the problem is these nipples. I bought another pack thinking I might have bought a dud, but still the same problem. I'm now using Advent instead and baby loves those bottles. No more choking or gas. Review 7: rating 1.0. not good these nipples actually collapsed when my son was drinking his bottle with these nipples. i kept having to pull the bottle out of his mouth so that the nipple would return to its original shape. Review 8: rating 1.0. Nipple is not as advertised Nipple is not as advertised, the flange is narrower then on the picture and it didn't fit well at all. Review 9: rating 1.0. stay away from these! I have been using dr browns bottles and nipples from week 6 to week 12 and my baby moved happily back and forth between bottle and boob. Then I got to use these (they are handy indeed, you only need to screw them on the medela pump bottle), well, after 3 times I used them my little one refused to latch on the breast. As far as I'm concerned, this is not a low flow nipple and it doesn't suite breastfed babies. Review 10: rating 1.0. Does not fit all bottles I ordered these nipples so they would have a slow flow for my 1 month old baby. They stated they fit all Medela bottles, NOT TRUE! These are too wide and will not fit my bottles. What a waste of money! I'd have to buy more bottles to make them fit and I have no idea which bottles to buy that they would fit. Review 11: rating 1.0. Not the same as pictured, does not fit Medela collars I just purchased the "Medela Breastmilk Feeding Storage & Collection Bottle (5 oz/150 ml) BPA/DEHP Free - (Set of 6)" from Amazon a few weeks ago. I thought it would be nice to have some extra nipples, so I ordered these. The picture shows the same nipples that are in the set, and the description says they fit all Medela bottles. When they arrived, I realized the product I received does not look like the product pictured o... Review 12: rating 4.0. Needs a Very Slow Option These are great, but they need a very slow option. My little guy is a strong newborn and just gulps the milk down like there is no resistance from the nipple at all. Review 13: rating 2.0. No Collars I needed a few extra nipples for our Medela bottles, and while these would work well they don't have the collars with them. That means that I still have no more extra sets than I did before. Review 14: rating 5.0. Works well, baby latches well. My wife and I have been pleased with these nipples. We bought them to use with the bottles that came with her Medela pump that only had the storage caps. We bought this and the Medela Breastmilk Bottle Spare Parts bag. Our lactation consultant had recommenced a slow flow nipple to avoid baby developing a preference for the bottle over the breast (which may occur with a faster flow nipple that makes eating for bottle easier). These Medela nipples are eas... Review 15: rating 5.0. Life savior for my 2 week old The product arrived on time and was a savior for me. My 2 week old had to be bottle fed along with being breast fed and after trying several brands and reading reviews, this was the find for him that worked.

Of course I was worried about nipple confusion but this nipple did not create that for my son. I was also worried about volume confusion and had tried the SNS as suggested by the lactation consultant but that was impractical beyond a week.... Review 16: rating 2.0. Two Stars Still too fast for my 2 month old Review 17: rating 2.0. Flow really isn't that slow. Ended up buying Nuk ... Flow really isn't that slow. Ended up buying Nuk orthodontic bottles which have worked well when supplementing breastfeeding with bottle feeding expressed milk. Review 18: rating 1.0. DEFINITELY not slow flow! This nipple is DEFINITELY not slow flow! It comes out way too fast. My baby was having a hard time taking the bottle, so I tested the nipple by running water through it. It comes out very fast. Not sure why they don't just make a slow flow nipple. Don't buy these if you want a true slow flow. It could affect your baby's ability to latch on to the breast. Review 19: rating 3.0. My baby took them......... reluctantly At first, my daughter didn't care for these. I have a very fast letdown and these actually made her work harder for the milk, so of course she preferred the breast. After a few days, however, the sitter was able to get her to take them. Now she takes them without a problem. Review 20: rating 2.0. Two Stars These nipples flow too quickly for my baby and constantly collapse.

I have given up on putting really bad diapers in the pale because of the smell and the lack of working ability of these small paper weights! Try something else like deer urine before you buy these things! Review 7: rating 2.0. Cute but that does not make it work. I was hoping to use them in our bathroom where we have the pee pads for the puppies as well... but no luck there either.

Gave two stars because the size is nice...

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. Lasted 48 hours. If your child likes to chew their bottles/sippy cups do not get this! After the first day my toddler chewed it where it was no longer leak proof. At the end of the second day he chewed the whole tip off. (The center part of it) so it was basically just an open bottle at that point and had to be thrown away. Waste of money. Review 2: rating 2.0. Too stiff Idea and design are good but my 18 month old can't get any water out of the firm plastic top. I even tried and it would take an industrial vacuum to suck liquid out. Very disappointed, especially given the other good reviews. Review 3: rating 1.0. Silicone easy to bite through Not impressed. If your kid chews on sippy cups/bottles, don't buy this one. My son bit the soft silicone tip almost completely off during the first use. Also, the holes in the tip are very restrictive and don't let much liquid through when trying to drink (which is probably why he was chewing on it). It is a cute cup, but if it can't stand up to a toddler, it is useless. Review 4: rating 5.0. I HAVE 20. I love them that much. It seems we can't buy enough of these cups. After using them for 2 years we still have the originals but keep buying more. If you want a cup that is truly leak free, these are it. I hand wash them every time, I don't use the clip, it easily comes off. What's left is easily disassembled and washed. MOLD FREE. There is no place for mold to hide. Review 1: rating 1.0. Works good for first couple months The screen blacks out after 20 min Review 2: rating 5.0. Five Stars Such a great monitor for the price! Definitely recommend! Review 3: rating 5.0. Love this monitor Love this monitor! It is a good quality video. The extra features like playing songs music or sounds and the ability to talk to my baby from the monitor is really helpful I definitely recommend this to anyone Review 4: rating 3.0. Overall a decent buy, but not perfect. Overall not the absolute best buy, but let’s be honest it’s a $50 product and does about what you’d expect. It’s worth it over these $500 camera setups that tell you every metric about the baby’s sleep... c’mon. If you expect perfection, then the issues below may bother you enough to opt for another item.

PROS:
Inexpensive
Battery lasts 2 hours disconnected from power
Good audio quality From cam to monitor
Still wo... Review 5: rating 1.0. Great monitor, except... Edit: after exactly 2 months of using the monitor the monitor went white and you can't see anything. I can still hear my baby but a white screen doesn't help much.

Overall pretty happy with this monitor. Downfalls: the 1000' range definitely isn't close. I can be two rooms over or down the hall (maybe 35' away) and it is beeping that I'm out of range. Also, after just a month of use, only at nighttime, it looks like one of the colored lights on th... Review 6: rating 1.0. 2 different issues with 2 diffrent units The first monitor we received had a faulty monitor. The picture had horizontal fuzzy lines through it. I exchanged it and the seller promptly sent a replacement. Last night I used the replacement and in the middle of the night it started to make a very loud white noise sound. The only way to stop the white noise was to completely turn off the sound. Needless to say I will be returning that system too. Review 7: rating 5.0. PERFECT! My daughter has seizures PERFECT! My daughter has seizures. Recently, she had a tonic-clinic seizure. She is old enough to want to sleep in her own room and bed. After this last seizure, I did not sleep for days. My husband bought this and I am able to see her as much as I want while she sleeps through the night. I finally slept when he plugged in this monitor and I could make sure she was safe. A baby needs a monitor but so many other people, for whatever reason, could use... Review 8: rating 5.0. Excellent picture. I use this to make sure my 104 year old aunt is okay. Now I have the freedom to go outside and not have to worry about her. It has an excellent picture. Great product. Review 9: rating 2.0. Fine picture and sound but doesn’t move This camera is not at all useful for us as you can’t control the camera position from the other troller. We have multiples, so we need a camera that can do this. Not at all worth the price, in my opinion. Review 10: rating 1.0. DON’T BUY THIS RUBISH! TRUST ME! IT’S A RUBISH! THE RANGE IS A BAD JOKE, JUST WORK WITH 5 METERS (I AM SERIOUS, JUST FI VE ME TERS), AFTER THIS ‘LOOOONG DISTANCE’, THE SIGNAL FALL COMPLETLY AND START BEEPING. TRUST ME, DO NOT BUY THIS RUBISH! Review 11: rating 1.0. No sound After 38 days I have no sound.. disappointed. Review 12: rating 4.0. Bright light. Okay monitor Good monitor overall, but a bit fuzzy at times. Also, this is so freaking bright if you’re checking baby at 3am with no option to turn brightness down Review 13: rating 1.0. Had a green light and was plugged into wall so I know it was charged. Read through manuals and you tube tutorials and could not get it working. Contacted Moto... Review 14: rating 5.0. reference_output: rating 2.0. No go I’ve had this for about a week or so and the day time it’s a great camera but I mainly got this for night time and this particular one really sucks with the night vision, it’s just blurry and all I see is my daughters hair it’s just a blob. I’m returning it.

Then when I do get a reading it’s always showing in the 97 degree range. I’ve added one degree to each reading. However, I’ve gone back to using the ear thermometer. More accurate, though costly to maintain. When I bought this, I ... Review 19: rating 1.0. Beautiful design, inaccurate readings I thoroughly read the instructions and followed them. I tried replacing the batteries and tried it on other people in the house so I could be sure I was holding it the proper distance from their forehead. Th... Review 20: rating 1.0. difficult to use it is difficult to use
persona: This customer prioritizes functionality and reliability in baby and child care products, with a strong preference for items that perform consistently and are built to last, while being willing to accept basic design or aesthetics if core functionality is solid.
reference_output:
rating 1.0. Everything is good but the accuracy This little thingy is well designed, but its accuracy is questionable. I could be taking a couple of temperatures a few seconds apart at the same location on the forehead, and the readings could be +/- 1 degrees. That's way too inaccurate - you can't really tell if you're having a low grade fever or a normal temperature.

Update: After 1 month of use, it no longer works. Always gives a 91-93 degree reading on the forehead. Tried to use alkaline batteries and rechargeable batteries, but didn't make a difference.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Great Video Monitor! Purchased this to monitor our toddler who will probably be climbing out of his crib soon. I chose the DXR-5+ based on the good reviews here.
The image quality seems quite good in both daytime and nighttime lighting. Even at night we can see enough detail to tell which of his stuffed animals he is cuddling with. Sound quality is good, too.
Setup was very easy. We put the camera on top of a tall dresser next to the crib. We have a fairly large house with LO's room at one end of the upper level, and have never had any issues with signal strength anywhere, including the basement and garage on the other side of the house.
The only complaint I have is that the battery doesn't last all night, but my guess is that is pretty typical for video monitors. We just generally leave it plugged in or switched to audio mode when we are using it at night. The cord is long enough that this isn't too much of an inconvenience.
Highly recommend this product!

Review 1: rating 2.0. too thick that it hurts to put on The cream is too thick and hurts while you try to apply it to sore nipples. However it is odorless and so better than other ones out there. Thick and effective This is the thickest of the lanolin creams that I've found, but it is also the most effective. I've also tried the Medela brand, which didn't really work for me. This brand is super thick, which creates a real barrier and makes it more comfortable to wear a bra with sore nipples. This brand is also safe for baby, so you don't need to wash it off before breastfeeding. Amazon's price is a good $2 less than I've found in stores. Review 3: rating 5.0. Use at night I put lanolin on my hands then gloves before I go to sleep at night. I wake up with well moisturized hands. I also put on lips for moisture. Since lanolin is thick and sticky, it stays where it is put. This lanolin does not smell like sheep. Review 4: rating 5.0. best for chapped lips I used to have chronic chapped lips. Now, evem in winter as long as I am using this lanolin i never have a problem. I even have my bf using it too!! Review 5: rating 5.0. Very good Love this lanolin though I'm not breastfeeding. It's a savior when the chapped lips are in the last stage when applying any regular chapstick burns. Too expensive to use it freely on other parts of the body but I still rate it 5 stars. Good to use on cracked newborns' skin like hands and feet. Review 6: rating 5.0. Best lip balm EVER-HG worthy i am not a breastfeeding mother. I buy this for lip balm. I started out using an old clarins formula that went discontinued, then found its twin in Bite Beauty's agave lip mask. But boy is that expensive for a tiny tube! Then in my birchbox I got a sample of Dr Lipp nipple balm for lips and it again was the same great stuff... Only ingredient being lanolin! Ding ding ding that's the common denominator here - the key ingredient in all my fave lip products.... Review 7: rating 5.0. This Cream is a Lifesaver! I breastfed my twins for 13 months and used this lanolin cream nearly every day, sometimes for treating tender nipples but mostly for preventing dry, cracked nipples. It was a lifesaver on more than one occasion! I didn't use other brands of lanolin creams so I really can't compare but I will say it worked so well for me. It is a bit thick but I think that's what helped me so much throughout those months. Also, because it's safe for a baby, I would put some... Review 8: rating 5.0. Cannot live without this! This is a must have for any new nursing or pumping mom. I couldn't live without it. And one tube goes a long way because all you need is a tiny pea sized amount. Its the perfect consistency, not too thin or too thick. It never bothered my son when he was nursing. Review 9: rating 1.0. Doesn't work! Doesn't work and dried my nipples!!!! I use this every day after my shower as well as after each time that I exfoliate my lips. It's incredibly soothing, I wish I had purchas... Review 12: rating 5.0. This is by far the best nipple cream you can purchase This is by far the best nipple cream you can purchase. I have purchased a couple different kind and this is the best. I cut back on using it as often, I now just use it after a shower to keep them hydrated or when I feel sore due to nursing and pumping all day.

rating 5.0. My favorite baby soap! We have been using this soap since our daughter was born and I just love it. The formula is gentle on her skin and leaves a very light scent that fades pretty quickly. It cleans off paint, dirt, food, markers, and whatever else a toddler gets on her in a normal day.

My only complaint is that these large bottles don’t come with a pump. They’re inconvenient to turn upside down and get especially so near the end. We bought a separate soap pump and that has made our lives so much easier.

For the soap itself, highly recommend.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. A must buy My son loves to put his head into the toilet bowl, which is very yucky and dirty. This safety lock works well for me, and my son cannot duck his head into the toilet bowl nor put his hand to swirl the toilet water anymore. Review 2: rating 1.0. P This item is NOT secure and does not serve its purpose. I actually would love if someone could post a video of this item serving it’s purpose as described. Review 3: rating 2.0. Incomplete and can’t install I received two sticks but only one safety hook. I need a second hook to prevent the stick from sliding Review 4: rating 2.0. Read before you buy Doesn’t work unless your toliet lid is COMPLETELY 100% FLAT!! If it has a curve design at all it will NOT work Review 5: rating 2.0. Didn’t stop my toddler Super easy to put on. However it doesn’t stop my toddler from lifting the lid still. The rubber end that goes against the tank just ends up sliding if you lift the lid with enough force. (My 17 month old can lift it.) I let the adhesion cure for 3 days before she started messing with it. The sticker ended up just popping right off. This did not do the trick for my household. Review 6: rating 5.0. Easy, inexpensive, and works! This lock works amazing! I bought the 2 pack to keep my 1 year old out of a couple of toilets that's she's curious about. The locks are easy to install, and easy to use. Just take off the backing to the adhesive, position it on the toilet seat so the bar is just barely not touching the toilet and press down. Touch the button and swing away until the bar locks, open toilet seat, do your business, close seat, press button and the bar swings back by itself.... Review 7: rating 2.0. Disliked Was not satisfied! Was not made very good! Did not work as well as I hoped! Review 8: rating 4.0. Place it snugly up to toilet! [[VIDEOID:fe0be59b09fb90c31df43a1c683ec4e1]] This is easy to use but can be overcome with slight effort (something a 1 year old could accomplish if highly motivated).

I placed my adhesive on the seat and tried it out, but was dissapointed. I wish I would have placed it tighter up to the toilet, as it was I had it just touching, it should be jammed up against it.

I tried to take it off but it was stuck on so very tight, I cannot get ... Review 9: rating 2.0. Baby hacked it within a week She just.... strong armed it. Review 10: rating 5.0. Toddler is Thwarted, boo hoo Our toddler started to get really interested in the toilet, just about as interested in the trashcan. We ordered these locks to prevent the eventual anything and everything goes in the toilet. These are simple and nearly universal for toilets. Install was easy and came with extra 3M sticky tabs and a hook if the toilet tank was sloped. We have these on a flat lid toilet with a curved tank and a curved lid with a flat tank, both work perfectly. The mechani... Review 11: rating 1.0. My 9 month old broke it off My son broke this off 3 days later. I would not recommend buying it if your kid is brutal like mine Review 12: rating 1.0. Doesn't lock It sticks well and if the lid is lifted, it stays closed. However.......my 1 year old just has to push on the bar ever so slightly and it opens and moves and then he can open the lid. It would be better to use the straps or to just keep the bathroom door closed. Review 13: rating 1.0. These are NOT toddler-proof. This is my second time trying to review this product. My first review was "denied".
Took my 17-month-old two days to figure this out. These do not work. They are way too easy to move the latch without even pushing down the button. Review 14: rating 2.0. Doesn’t work Did not work. My son easily unlocked it within seconds. Review 15: rating 5.0. Perfect! Perfect for keeping my son out of the toilet but easy enough for my toddler to use the bathroom unassisted. Happy I found something that finally worked after struggling to keep him out of the toilet, but her being able to access the toilet without help. Review 16: rating 1.0. Doesn’t stay on kids pull it right off My kid pulled it off the second day Review 17: rating 1.0. Not child proof Came off of the toilet way too easily even after I replaced the adhesive stickers still came off a second time and would not stick again Review 18: rating 1.0. Toilet seat If your toilet seat moves at all it won’t work Review 19: rating 2.0. Not as simple as seems, not effective I don't think it keeps the lid shut tightly enough, my 1.5 year old can open it enough to do put things into the toilet. Hard to know where to place. Review 20: rating 3.0. They didn't fit my toilets - returned I really like the design of these as compared to the other toilet locks for baby-proofing that are on the market. The product itself was sturdy, it was a great design, but unfortunately when I went to test it out, and plan where it was going to go, it didn't fit just right. When I put the device in place on the toilet lid and applied pressure to simply hold the device in place, the toilet lit opened when I tried even gently to open it. My toil...
persona: This customer prioritizes product safety and durability for young toddlers, favoring items that are effective and long-lasting during early developmental stages, and is attentive to design features that prevent easy access by curious children.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Great for younger toddler Works great for now at up to 2 but he is getting smart on how to open things.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 3.0. Potentially dangerous. Considering return after 5 months of use. We purchased two superyards and assembled them to gate off our livingroom area and allow for entry from two points. This product is excellent at keeping a baby in one spot or another. The gate is well built. It is easy to assemble and reconfigure.

It is also dangerous. Our boy (one year old now) can stick his arms and legs through the gaps in the slats of the fence. This may seem innocent at first, but ... Review 2: rating 1.0. Absolute garbage. Poor quality control from this company Absolute garbage. Poor quality control from this company. Gate button defective on arrival. Waste of time. Review 3: rating 5.0. Five Stars I brought it for my great grand daughter she loves it and so does her mommy Review 4: rating 4.0. Great product so far. I bought this to put around the area of my doggie door so the dogs can go in and out without running around the whole entire house. I do like that it was easy to put together and can be shaped to work just perfect around the area I am using it for. I originally was going to use it across the whole opening between my kitchen and living room, but changed my mind because the door is so tiny it was too much of a pain going through it. I thought it would be larger... Review 5: rating 5.0. LOVED this baby gate LOVED this baby gate! It's huge and versatile! We had one large combined room that served as a kitchen and dining area and we used this gate to section off the room in half. We turned the dining area into the play area. I could cook and clean and watch our son play while I was in the kitchen. Review 6: rating 4.0. Sturdy, Safe, Easy -- Just Really Small I love this product in that it is very sturdy, extremely easy to set up, and it keeps my baby safe so that I can do all those things that a person has to do in order to remain sane and keep her house from falling down around her. I can pour myself a bowl of cereal every morning, visit the bathroom when I need to, change the laundry over, even take the occasional shower without worrying that my little one will be chewing on lamp chords or tumbl... Review 7: rating 3.0. Did not remain stable in one place Didn't work for us. Kid was not even walking but was able to move it around the floor, or was able to change the shape by pulling/pushing. Had to return it Review 8: rating 4.0. Good sturdy, haven't used it as hexagon, but as a wall. Opens up. Can remove sections, but has to be even. Not Odd numbers. We have a LONG open living room and this works great. I wish the gate swung both directions. And it is a very difficult gate to open. But there's also not much competition in this area... even still works great. Review 9: rating 5.0. Excellent Pen: easy to assemble, sturdy, nothing for a dog to climb I bought this pen to replace the IRIS 8 panel containment pen. The IRIS is a great pen if you don't have a climber, but my Dachshund climbed it like a ladder. The North States pen has only vertical rails, nothing to climb. It's metal rather than plastic, so it's a bit heavier and not as easy to move around, but it seems to be a great product. Easy to assemble, including the wall mount hardware if desired. I had ... Review 10: rating 5.0. Connect two for 8'x4' baby jail! Perfect for twins! This product warrants its stellar rating. Thank goodness I did not purchase the plastic "Superyard" version! This gate has fulfilled all of my requirements: Not hideous, extremely strong/stable, spacious, not climbable, and tall. It has been a dream come true from the moment we set it up. In fact, we purchased two of them and constructed a 8'x4' baby jail. It's huge! My 8-month old twins have plenty of room to crawl around a... Review 11: rating 5.0. Solid construction and great value. The assembly instructions are poorly written and misleading. Each panel is 24 inches wide and has a joining post with a handle at the top. Turn to lock or release as indicated on the handle. The handle has a spring inside. Push down on the handle to shorten the post for removal/installation. There is also a slot cut at the bottom of the post to help with removal/installation.
I also purchased the two panel extension. It was quickly and e... Review 12: rating 5.0. Great - good for babies who want to pull up/climb Our son is now 8 months old, and we have been using this superyard for about two months or so now. It's awesome - very sturdy and stable. It won't fall over or be pulled down. The only down side I can see is it is hard when the poor tyke was just starting to sit up and pull up, because he kept conking his noggin on the metal sides as he fell over, and that wasn't much fun. We lined it with pillows to protect him for about a month, whi... Review 13: rating 5.0. Well worth the money. We bought this gate to close off an 11' span with a step down into the next room, so we're using it as a straight across gate instead of a play yard. This was the only gate I could find at that length out of the box without adding any sections and with the door at one end instead of the middle. It works great! The swinging gate section is a little small, but it's so easy to set up and break down that we just take it down when we have company over. The openin... Review 14: rating 5.0. Great gate but it does take up a lot of space when not in use I should be up front that although I love this and gave it five stars, we did return it. We bought it to pen in our now ten-month-old daughter and in the end it just took up too much space for it to work for our needs. It was really well made and incredibly sturdy. I liked how tall it was, too. We did purchase (and return) the two panel addition because this is pretty small otherwise. A really nice feature of the gate... Review 15: rating 5.0. great product i love this product. It is very steady and successfully seperate my dogs from TV. it works and I will recommend it. Review 16: rating 5.0. Excellent purchase We bought this gate to protect our 1 yr old from the woodstove. It works great!! It was very easy to use and is extremely sturdy without attaching it to the walls. I definitely recommend this to everyone! Review 17: rating 5.0. Awesome for my 6 Month old!! My baby girl is growing faster than expected. She outgrew her bouncers and swings because she started sitting up early. She couldn't stay still in a standard traditional playard alone. At 5 months she began standing up really early. This gate has exceeded my expectations and with that said, I'm able to do house work and school work with 2 kids, in a safer and more convenient manner. This was worth the money. Review 18: rating 4.0. Good gate, but there is a design flaw! We purchased this gate to separate our toddler's play area from the dining room. It is solid, sturdy, and looks like it will hold up for a long time. However, please read reviewer R. Harry's take on the design flaw! Since we were using three of the six panels (attached to the wall), it took a while to figure out how to disconnect/connect the appropriate pieces together. R. Harry explained the issue much better than I could.

This i... Review 19: rating 5.0. GREAT PRODUCT - Here is what you need to know about this gate Easy assembly, very sturdy, easy to add extension, gate panel works great. Our baby is totally secure when inside.

As soon as we put it together with the extension panels we ordered another set to make it twice as big. At 6 or even 8 panels it creates a pretty small space (a little bigger than a pack and play) which would be perfect for shorter time frames but we wanted a larger, longer stay place for our daught... Review 20: rating 5.0. Extremely well-made, worth the money. After reading all the product reviews, we sprung for two of these gates and put them together yesterday. We have them configured in a square with three segments per side, for a total of 36' of internal square footage. The gate is very sturdy and well-made. It is a putty color, and looks very nice in our home. We are thrilled with it so far. We want an entirely safe play space for our son who is now 6 months old and will probably be crawling soon....
persona: This customer values practicality, durability, and ease of use in products designed for active lifestyles, particularly those that solve specific functional challenges related to travel and pet or child safety, and they prioritize well-constructed, adaptable solutions that deliver consistent performance over time.
reference_output: rating 5.0. So I had to make it smaller and covered it with one of the other panels and it worked perfect. My smaller chihuahua of 3 lbs fit I've had this play yard for about 3 years and have zero complaints. I bought it initially for my chihuahua Mickey but that wasn't very successful as he jumped out as soon as I would leave for work! So I had to make it smaller and covered it with one of the other panels and it worked perfect. My smaller chihuahua of 3 lbs fits in through the bars with no problems though haha! Anyway now we have a baby and he's 1 year old and he will now be using it as a play yard for him. So now my fur babies need a new play yard also! Haha. I would definitely recommend this play yard it's very sturdy and it is perfect!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. More temperamental than my two year old I've had this two weeks and I've already unscrewed it twice to try to figure out how to get it working again.  It goes from not rotating at all to spinning so fast it should have a seizure warning.  If there is a pattern to the buttons and what they do, I have no idea what it is.  Trying to get the setting with all the lights that my daughter likes involves randomly clicking buttons and hoping.  I've taped the outside because it keeps my daught...

Review 2: rating 1.0. Junk This is total junk. I bought it for my kids. I assumed it would light up and reflect on the ceiling and walls but the light isn't strong enough to go more than a few inches above the globe. If you expect to see stars on your ceiling room, DO NOT BUY THIS. It won't happen. I assume it's made for someone just to look at the pretty colors and lights ON the globe itself (??), otherwise it's useless. I returned this item.

Review 3: rating 2.0. Two Stars It's an OK product but the cord does not stay in very well

Review 4: rating 3.0. Great functionality for the price, but motors are the weak point. For the price and the amount of enjoyment my 2 kids get out of it as a nightlight, I would have rated this 4.5 stars (5 stars if the stars used clearer plastic). Why the 3 stars? Well, one of the 2 globes I purchased (in June) stopped rotating a month after purchase, and the other one (bought in Feb) just stopped rotating today. I'm listening to my 2 year old complain about the lack of rotation on the baby monitor as I...

Review 5: rating 1.0. Very cheap quality. The lights are only red or ... Very cheap quality. The lights are only red or green and are not bright enough to hit the ceiling of my daughter's room. If you turn on the spin feature, the lights are much dimmer then go out completely.

Review 6: rating 1.0. Just recently the third light gave out and now it is useless. I'm forced to throw it out After 3 months of use, one of the three lights gave out.  A month later the second light gave out.  Just recently the third light gave out and now it is useless.  I'm forced to throw it out.  My son has a nightlight we got as a gift for our baby shower, he is now 5 years old and it still works! We gave that one to his baby sister as he wanted a big boy light, which is why we bought this one and n...

Review 7: rating 2.0. They are nice though. My son loves it Not fancy. Don't expect to be overwhelmed by the visual effects. They are nice though. My son loves it. However, after three months it stopped rotating. :(

Review 8: rating 5.0. I love it! I have to play it in order ... I love it! I have to play it in order to know the functions of each buttons and it is really cool. If you hold the buttons for quite a while then it will make the kinda on/off motion making it look like it is dancing. This would be a great gift for girls room. Fantastic!

Review 9: rating 1.0. Super cheap. Broke too quickly. Super cheap.

Review 10: rating 5.0. So happy I got this for him I bought this for my sons room ( he is 4 ) He LOVES it! It helps with keeping the room lit but not too much and the different varieties of color combos and the fact it rotates makes it that much cooler. So happy I got this for him.

Review 11: rating 2.0. Misrepresentation of product and accessories. No AC cord, so I spent more in batteries than it was worth. The colors are pretty and all of the ways to change the image was great, it just sucked away all of the batteries we used. If it was meant to run solely on batteries, then they should of made it more efficient. I wasted my money. It will be given to Goodwill and i am searching for a better quality cosmic nightlight that plays actual music and comes with an outlet cord.

Review 12: rating 1.0. Wouldn't buy again Worked fine the first night. After that, it kind of just works when it wants. I've tried brand new batteries and plugging it in...one time I'll push a  button and it does one function, when I push it again, it does another. Most of the time, it doesn't work at all. Wouldn't buy again.

Review 13: rating 2.0. Two Stars Broke after less than 3 months of use.

Review 14: rating 1.0. Do not buy Did not like it at all, looks really good and right on photo not how it actually is. Very disappointed!!!

Review 15: rating 5.0. Five Stars very pleased, would defiantly purchase again. very bright love how it changes colors. well worth the price.

Review 16: rating 2.0. Not a long lasting product It was nice for a short while, then after 6 months it stopped rotating, the green light stoped working then the red, now it’s just blue and white.

Review 17: rating 3.0. I guess great if you have usb outlets in your home I would give this 5 stars if it came with a wall plug, just a cord with a usb adapter. I have to plug it into my phone charger usb plug or go buy a plug. I guess great if you have usb outlets in your home.

Review 18: rating 1.0. One Star Very dull and cheap plastic.

Review 19: rating 2.0. Dollar store quality No auto shutoff so it will run all night unless you physically remember to unplug it, a bit noisy, short USB plug limits placement, no DC plug (I just used an old phone charger), a strange sort of film inside the globe, crappy instructions and labelling, no memory on poorly labelled buttons. This would probably cost $4 at a dollar store or Chinese market. That said, the stars and moons look pretty good on our daughter's ceiling.

Review 20: rating 5.0. Well worth the money - we love it. Love love love it!  We set it up in our baby's (8months) room and his eyes lit up.  It provides enough light at night that we don't step on toys and in the morning when he wakes up giggling as he tries to catch the stars on the wall.  It's priceless!  We have it plugged in with a USB adaptor & it saves so many batteries. U can run it all night and doesn't even get warm to touch which is a plus.  As an extra bonus the dog loved it as well, he didn't ...
persona: This customer prioritizes quality and safety in baby products, especially favoring glass over plastic for health concerns, and values customization and aesthetic appeal, but is highly sensitive to poor craftsmanship and material comfort, often returning or repurposing items that fail to meet functional or tactile standards.
reference_output: rating 1.0. I really loved this light so much that I bought a second ... I really loved this light so much that I bought a second one for my other child. If you purchase this, please check on it frequently or don't leave it on during the night. I left it on and told my son good night. My husband went in a few minutes later to say goodnight and it was smoking!!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 2.0. Not worth the money I was very excited getting this product but my son was not interested in it in the least. Very disappointed. I will not recommend this product to anyone not to mention the price for it is ridiculous. Review 2: rating 2.0. Dissapointed Bought this toy after reading all the great reviews but my daughter didn't like it. She is not drawn to it at all. Waste of money. I gave it two stars because is cute, safe and I'm hoping she will change her mind in the future. Review 3: rating 2.0. don't waste your money...unless you have a YORKIE! My son has never been interested in this! I offered it too him since he was 2 months old and he is now 11 months old and has 8 teeth and has never once used it as a teether.....On the other hand, I have to constantly discipline my yorkie dog to keep her from getting it....The squeaker also keeps me up at night when he rolls over onto it so I can't keep it in the crib with him. Every time I see the thing I think to myself..."what a ... Review 4: rating 1.0. One Star My boy didnt like it...he just ignored it Review 5: rating 1.0. Possible fake Sophie Don't buy this. I thought I found a great deal but after doing some research it really looks like this is a knock off. Everything looks legit on the surface, but after buying a real Sophie in a trusted store, I compared the two and noticed obvious differences. If you look online you can get info about how to spot a fake Sophie. Long story short, if you're going to buy your baby the world's most expensive teething toy, just bite the bullet and buy it. Don't t... Review 6: rating 1.0. Crushed box Box crushed. It was a gift. Review 7: rating 1.0. So expensive and overrated A waste of money. My baby hardly uses it and it was so expensive! I would not recommend and don't understand the hype Review 8: rating 2.0. nothing special for the price I bought it just because it is a best seller. My baby likes it fine but its nothing special, its just like any other teether or rattle, they enjoy having some in their mouth specially when its new. in my experience, very young babies like best the toy you give them most to. Review 9: rating 5.0. Sophie teether I purchased this little guy for my 5 mo. old granddaughter. She was immediately drawn to it and has enjoyed "teething" on it from the minute it was introduced to her.
We had no issues with the feet choking. And she loved that she occasionally was greeted with a "squeak" from Sophie.
We love this product & recommend this product. Review 10: rating 5.0. Good product, I hope my baby will like it Good product,I hope my baby will like it. Review 11: rating 2.0. Two Stars Overrated? Everyone has one but our little one wasn't fond of it. Review 12: rating 5.0. Sophie Very fast delivery! I can't wait to give it to my new neice!
Thank you! Review 13: rating 2.0. Disappointed I don't understand all the five star review for Sophie. It's cute, but only dollar store cute. As in, I would pay one, maybe two dollars for it. It's smaller than I thought it would be and it just looks cheap. My baby likes it a little bit, but would rather play with an empty water bottle. Review 14: rating 1.0. One Star My 6 month old almost choked on the leg!! Review 15: rating 2.0. Sophie is incredible Everyone on here was right. Sophie is incredible. Sophie can win a game of connect four in only three moves. When Alexander Bell invented the telephone he had 3 missed calls from Sophie. Seriously, our boy loves this thing. He enjoys chewing on it and she is the perfect size for his little hands.

Update: This is an overpriced over hyped little fella. She is quality but far too expensive. Thus I am lowering the stars from five to two. She was quickly to... Review 16: rating 1.0. Glorified dog toy This is a glorified dog toy, it feels like one, it squeaks like one, I thought it was something special from all of the good reviews but it is not. You can buy 3x the amount of good teethers for the price. Honestly people were asking why my son had a dog toy. Review 17: rating 4.0. A little overrated Seeing the glowing review on Amazon, I caved in to the hype and bought 1 for my daughter when she was a few months old (she's now nearly 2). She enjoyed it and played with it for a while and as with any kids toy, it is now sitting in the closet of "forgotten friends". I don't regret purchasing it, but one thing I want to note is that the color all faded away within a few months of use. Sophie is now an albino giraffe... Review 18: rating 4.0. Pricey but worth it! My son is 3 months old and recently he started teething(i know!!!!). While I was not even thinking about teethers I needed to buy one which my son could use. I hopped on to amazon to search for a teether n Sophie the giraffe was the top rated with over 1500 reviews albeit a little pricey.I decided to buy it just because it was rated so high and boy was I was pleasantly surprised! I bought two more tethers but my son liked Sophie a lot more! The other teether [[AS... Review 19: rating 2.0. Very disappointed I was a litte uncertain about the price on this item at first, but it did receive several good reviews and I thought it would be a great teether. I was wrong, my granddaughter never took to it nor did she want to play with it either. It reminds me of a bad dog toy made out of rubber. For the cost, texture, and size it is not worth it. Review 20: rating 2.0. Giraffe Wow this toy sure is anoying! a friend of ours told us it was they best toy for teething child.. Our child really didn't like it
persona: This customer prioritizes product safety and durability for infants, values functionality during travel, and is willing to accept minor flaws like difficult cleaning if the item performs well over time; they appreciate thoughtful design features such as a protective cover and are influenced by real-world usability and long-term reliability rather than price.
reference_output: rating 1.0. not sure if it was the authentic one or not returned it because of the fraud alert going on.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. SOO much space! LOOOOVE this bag. The NEW brown is the second one I have (Black Original, 1st). I absolutely love the update with the pocket in back, the handle on top, and the seatbelt like straps (makes it look fancy)!!! I hope they make a purple color one day! I know some reviews mentioned theirs fell apart, but luckily I have yet to experience that. The black backpack I have had since June, and took it on a 2 week road trip. Hopefully this one will hold up too. Review 2: rating 5.0. Comfortable, roomy, and super stylish! I am loving this diaper bag! Very roomy with pockets for anything you’ll need. They updated the straps and now they’re really comfortable, so I appreciate that. The leather almost looks real and I’ve already gotten so many compliments on it!! Cannot recommend enough!! Review 3: rating 4.0. I prefer the MF store bag Let me start this by saying that I spent alot of time debating whether to order the MF bag (sister company) or the Miss Fong, they look almost identical but with a 5-10 dollar price difference. I ended up ordering both just to check them out!
First, the Miss Fong bag I wouldn't have kept regardless. It arrived with damage "spots" on the gold metal hardware (like you might see over time or with water damage), where the gold cover has worn off. Photo show... Review 4: rating 5.0. Best multifunction bag ever! I love this bag! I am a childless, 20-something teacher who uses this as a work bag. It has enough storage for all of my junk! The insulated drink pockets, ability to wear as a backpack or tote, and the amount of pockets were huge selling points for me. Several colleagues have complimented the bag and can’t believe it when I explain that it is a diaper bag. Recommended for everyone! Review 5: rating 5.0. Highly recommend! I have owned and been using this diaper bag for 2 months.

PROS:
•Quality of the bag (feels sturdy and looks well-made)
•Compartments inside the main bag (two insulated pockets, mesh pockets allow you to see contents)
•Outside pockets (easy access)
•Zipper feature that allows the bag to be placed over luggage for travel.
•Changing pad is a bonus, but I will probably never use it.
•Easy to wipe clean (outside) I’ve yet to need ... Review 6: rating 2.0. Don't buy unless you expect it to fall apart fast!!! I was so happy when I received this in the mail yesterday but quickly realized the material was cheap. I used the bag for two hours going to the store and it already has a scratch on the material on the front of the bag!!!!!! I can't believe it already looks used within hours. I should have bought a Fawn Design probably better material than this. SO buyer beware if you don't want it to hold up!!! Review 7: rating 2.0. Cute but zipper is cheap Cute and stylish but zipper is broken less than 6 months later with minimal use. You get what you pay for. Review 8: rating 3.0. Bag was used not new as indicated. There was a used chapstick in the diaper bag which proves item was not new as indicated. Love the bag but don’t like being deceived! Review 9: rating 2.0. Zipper broke in 1 month Only had it a month and main zipper is starting to break. Aside from that it’s great, but having a functional zipper is necessary for the bag to even have use. Review 10: rating 5.0. The search ends here! Buy it!! Have you searched hours and hours for the perfect diaper/mom bag?! Are you over it and just want something cute AND functional? Well look no further sister, this is it!

I have been stalking my delivery status (was delayed) for this bag to finally come in. I am so happy with it. It’s gorgeous and has the best interior storage setup. The brown is so nice and sleek with my black frame bugaboo donkey stroller. It’s love y’all! (Side note: it does... Review 11: rating 1.0. Not very durable I really love the look of this bag and the amount of space but I have had it less than a week and one of the straps already broke. I didn’t have much in it at the time so I guess the amount of storage doesn’t really matter if the straps can’t handle the weight. Review 12: rating 5.0. BUY THIS! Just do it! Wow! I am obsessed with this bag. I originally wanted a Fawn Design bag really bad but just couldn’t pay that much for it. I had another amazon diaper bag that was great as well but then it got stolen from my car so when I was shopping for a new diaper bag I knew I wanted to give this one a try. It is a great size, has so much storage capability, and it is soooo pretty! I’m so excited about it. Review 13: rating 1.0. Broken zipper The zipper broke 6 months into having it. Would appreciate a replacement bag. Review 14: rating 1.0. Missing stuff I bought the large diaper backpack because it said that it came with the changing pad but it was actually missing. I was so disappointed that i had to returned it after giving my old diaper bag away. Review 15: rating 1.0. Cute, but not practical I was super excited to get this bag, and then I started to pack it... if any of the pockets are used the inside of the bag has no room. Review 19: rating 5.0. Takes a while to apply but looks awesome above my toddlers crib Bright colors, big and small letters and animals for every sounds. persona: This customer prioritizes durability, functionality, and long-term usability in products for young children, values easy maintenance and safety features like adjustable height and secure straps, and is willing to invest in high-quality items that withstand frequent use and remain effective over time.
reference_output: rating 2.0. Attractive Giving a low rating only because of the struggle to get these to actually adhere to the wall. It’s a terrific set , bright colours, cute, my son loves counting the owls. I truly cannot imagine these peeling paint off your walls unless youve used crayons to paint your walls. It took me 3 attempts to get these to stick to the wall. 1st was peel & stick. Slowly they just start falling. 2nd time i used the white scotch brand double tape. Well they fell off that tape. Most recently i used the heavy duty black scotch brand double padded tape & it seemed to have worked. If you live in a hot climate i believe you will have the same problem. Otherwise, fantastic educational product once you spend days , months getting it up!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Functional This bag does what is expected. We flew to Florida and packed my 9mo son's rear facing Chicco KeyFit 30 seat and base in this. While it is awkward to put on your back because of the car seat inside, it was great to have my hand free to carry the rest of our luggage. I was skeptical at first of the product thinking that it wouldn't survive the baggage handlers at the airport, but it came thru unscathed as did everything we packed inside. I would recommend this to anyone loo... Review 2: rating 5.0. Worked very well. I bought this because of the reviews saying it wouldn't fall apart. They were right, it held up fine.

I didn't think I'd like the backpack style straps and thought I'd prefer a single shoulder strap. I was wrong. It was great over the shoulders -- the car seat was light enough there for me to focus on carrying/pulling other bags.

UPDATE: Baby's grown a bit and we bought a Britax Frontier 85 SICT. This bag does NOT fit the SICT, which is ve... Review 3: rating 4.0. Great buy Great quality! It fit my daughter's Britax Marathon car seat perfectly.

The only drawback is that you have to be careful when you wear it on your back. It's large and you're bound to bump into things behind you. Review 4: rating 5.0. Very Well Built bag Took this on our first Vacation with the little one. The Cosco Scenera Car Seat fit very easily in here, and there was room to pack additional items in there as well. The bag is very well padded, and we checked the bag and carseat both flights, and the bag held up well. The backpack straps are adjustable, and makes it easy to carry. Review 5: rating 4.0. Worth the money I was nervous about checking our Chicco Keyfit infant seat; four flights in a week meant countless baggage handlers tossing it here and there and lots of bumping around in the cargo area. The travel bag not only protected the seat, which came out damage-free, but it made it easy to transport AND was large enough to hold some extra stuff. Since the car seat checks for free, we tossed some extra diapers and wipes and a pair of my husband's shoes in there too. By the end... Review 6: rating 5.0. Works great, really large We bought this for our first plane trip with car seat (infant carrier, Chicco Keyfit 30). It worked great, survived the plane trip well. For the infant seat, it was quite roomy. We stuffed other items in as well, since you can check a car seat for free. We didn't really try out the backpack straps on this trip, but I can imagine they would be helpful. Review 7: rating 2.0. issues on 1 of 2 travel bags We purchased two of these "Ultimate" car seat bags, and have experienced a problem with one of them.

After just a single flight, for unknown reasons, the zipper pull will no longer "zip." Instead, when you move the zipper pull, the two sides of the zipper remain open. A car seat bag that won't zip close is useless.

I also found that even though this bag promised to fit our Recaro ProRide car seat, we couldn't make the travel bag wo... Review 8: rating 3.0. Pretty Good We ordered 2 of these to bring on our trip recently. Of the 2, one made it through without damage. The other was damaged on our first leg of the trip. When we got it from baggage claim, they had inspected both seats. One was fine, the other had a broken zipper. I'm sure it has something to do with the inspection people not being very gentle, but to have 2 brand new bags and one gets broken on our first use - kind of upsetting. We still had to use it to come back home.

We bought this and the plain red JL Childress Gate Check bag when our first kid was born. We have never used the gate check bag because this ultimate car seat travel bag is so much better. Why? Mainly because of... Review 16: rating 5.0. Solid Product - Great Value This is a great travel accessory for those who are bringing a car seat along. We recently visited Arizona with the family and needed to bring ours. After deliberating over several models, we decided to go with the JL Childress backpack model. If your back is good, this is a great way to get to the airport without having to lug the seat on wheels or attached to another bag.

Pros:

Lightweight - Not a lot of extra needless material

It does zip up but is impossible to carry because it jabs you in the back. A rolling bag would be a better choice -- wish I could find one! Anyone know of one that does fit? I wish someone would've posted this review ... Review 20: rating 5.0. Did the job. Fits Britax B-safe with room to spare Took this on a recent trip across the country.
